tasks.title: Data Engineer: Auto-merge new links and visuals on duplicate properties in sync pipeline. Currently, sync_data.js uses upsert logic (matched on address+area) that correctly preserves analyst fields (alpha_score, analyst_notes) but SILENTLY DROPS new data: (1) New links from a re-seen property are discarded — the UPDATE statement never touches the links column; (2) New gallery images, floorplan_url, and streetview_url from fresh scrapes are also dropped; (3) Higher-resolution image_url is not promoted. Fix: (a) On duplicate detection, merge new links into the existing links array (deduplicate by URL) before UPDATE; (b) Add missing gallery images to the existing gallery array; (c) Update image_url if new value is ≥1024px and existing is lower resolution or missing; (d) Add missing floorplan_url and streetview_url. The UPDATE statement in sync_data.js (line ~211) must be expanded to include these merge-logic fields. Preserve all existing analyst-calculated fields (alpha_score, appreciation_potential, analyst_notes, archived, archive_reason) — do not touch them.
notes: Root cause: sync_data.js UPDATE statement (line ~211) never touches links/gallery/image_url/floorplan_url — new data from re-seen properties is silently dropped.
Manual duplicate enrichment is now documented in agents/data_analyst/README.md (Duplicate Detection section).
This task automates the same logic in the sync pipeline for automated scrapes.

2026-04-02: DE-164 completed — UPDATE statement expanded with merge logic: (a) links merged + deduped by URL, (b) gallery merged + deduped, (c) image_url promoted if new >=1024px resolution, (d) floorplan_url/streetview_url filled in if missing. Existing analyst fields (alpha_score, analyst_notes, archived, archive_reason) fully preserved. Console logging for merge events added.

tasks.title: UI: Make loan term configurable on the Affordability Settings page. Currently `termYears` is hardcoded to 25 years in `useAffordability.ts` (useState(25)) and the Loan Term panel in `AffordabilitySettings.tsx` shows a static non-interactive placeholder ('Standard 25-Year Term'). (1) In `useAffordability.ts`: change `[termYears]` from `useState(25)` to `[termYears, setTermYears]` settable state. Persist `termYears` to localStorage under a `propSearch_loan_term` key. Expose `setTermYears` via an `updateTermYears` callback in the hook return. (2) In `AffordabilitySettings.tsx`: replace the static '25 Years' display in the Loan Term section (line 259) with an interactive segmented control or dropdown allowing the user to select from [15, 20, 25, 30] year terms. Wire `updateTermYears` to the control. The selected term should persist across sessions. (3) All downstream calculations (getAffordablePrice, getBudgetProfile, calculateMortgageFromPayment) already accept `years` as a parameter and are correctly reactive — no logic changes needed. (4) Add term-year selector to `BudgetSlider` if it is present on the same page. Verify all payment scenarios recalculate in real-time when term changes.

UI Refactor: Make MortgageTracker (/mortgage) a read-only intelligence page. Per user directive, Mortgage Intelligence is purely for reading market data. All personal input controls (monthlyBudget, mortgageTerm, LTV band) belong exclusively on AffordabilitySettings (/affordability). Remove from MortgageTracker.tsx: (1) `const [monthlyBudget, setMonthlyBudget] = useState(4500)` and `const [mortgageTerm, setMortgageTerm] = useState(25)` state declarations (lines 34-35). (2) The Monthly Budget number input in the Global Control Bar (lines 155-169). (3) The Loan Term number input in the Global Control Bar (lines 170-184). (4) The non-functional 'Execute Simulation' button (lines 576-578) — no onClick handler exists, dead UI. (5) Any `ppiHistory` calculations or `calculateMaxLoan` calls that depend on monthlyBudget/mortgageTerm — these personal affordability metrics belong on AffordabilitySettings. Keep: (a) LTV band selector (activeLTV) — this is a chart display filter for which market rate to show, not a personal setting. (b) Series visibility toggles (visibleSeries). Keep the Rate Corridor chart, PPI chart, and LTV Arbitrage Matrix — these display market data only. The charts should display the current live rate without any affordability personalisation. Update the page subtitle from 'Purchasing Power Simulation' to 'Market Rate Intelligence' or similar. Update the PPI chart label that references monthlyBudget ('Estimated Loan Ceiling @ £X/mo') to show the current live 5yr fixed rate benchmark without personal budget inputs.

tasks.title: UI: Reduce London metro line weight and opacity on all map views. tasks.title: Runtime React error in MarketConditionsBar.tsx:141 — 'Objects are not valid as a React child (found: object with keys {value, source, source_url, methodology, last_refreshed})'. Root cause: `data.sdlt_countdown` is a provenance-wrapped object but is being rendered directly as a React child. The `sdlt_countdown` field in `macro_trend.json` has the provenance schema `{value, source, source_url, methodology, last_refreshed}` but `MarketConditionsBar` renders it as a plain string. Fix: use `extractValue(data.sdlt_countdown)` or `data.sdlt_countdown?.value` before rendering.
notes: Confirmed via browser console: 'Objects are not valid as a React child' at MarketConditionsBar.tsx:141.
The provenance schema wraps values in objects — e.g. `sdlt_countdown: {value: '12 weeks', source: '...'}`.
MarketConditionsBar renders `{data.sdlt_countdown}` directly as text, which fails when it's an object.
Fix: use `extractValue(data.sdlt_countdown)` or `data.sdlt_countdown?.value ?? data.sdlt_countdown` before rendering.
All provenance-wrapped fields in the macro schema should be checked for this issue.

tasks.title: PropertyContext.tsx uses hardcoded `http://localhost:3001/api` instead of relative `/api` — fails in Playwright sandbox (localhost blocked from localhost). Root cause of 'Property loading error: Failed to fetch'. Fix: change line 39 from `'http://localhost:3001/api'` to `'/api'` to match useFinancialData.ts.
notes: Confirmed via smoke test console errors.
PropertyContext.tsx line 39: `const API_BASE = IS_DEMO ? '/data' : 'http://localhost:3001/api'`
useFinancialData.ts line 8 (correct): `const API_BASE = IS_DEMO ? '/data' : '/api'`
Playwright sandbox blocks direct localhost:3001 requests.
Vite proxy forwards /api → localhost:3001 — only works with relative paths.

Scope: (1) Add a structured market_status column to the properties table: ALTER TABLE properties ADD COLUMN market_status TEXT DEFAULT 'unknown' CHECK(market_status IN ('active','under_offer','sold_stc','sold_completed','withdrawn','unknown')); (2) For each of the 31 archived records, review the archive_reason and assign the correct market_status: 'Off-Market — Let Agreed/Listing Removed/Wrong Channel/New Build Sold/Stale/Student Let' → 'withdrawn'; 'Off-Market — Reclassified Overseas' → 'withdrawn'; 'Cannot Verify — Discard' (off-market reasons) → 'withdrawn'; 'Pre-enrichment Duplicate' → 'unknown' (duplicate, not a market signal); 'Still Active — Rightmove RES_BUY' (5 records) → 'active'; 'Data Discrepancy' → 'unknown'; (3) Populate market_status for ALL active (non-archived) records as 'active' — properties in the pipeline that are still listed are by definition active; (4) Document the taxonomy in agents/data_analyst/README.md under a new 'Market Status Classification' section; (5) Run a verification query to confirm all 73 records have a market_status value.
notes: Gated on DE-165 — needs market_status column to exist before populating it.
Root cause: no structured market_status field — analyst archive_reason is free text, not actionable in UI.
5 'Still Active' records (archived=1, archive_reason contains 'Still Active') are the priority: they need market_status='active' so FE-185 can surface them for rechecking.
New field will be used by FE-185 (Archive Review page) to group and filter archived records.
Also see: DE-165 (schema migration + sync pipeline), FE-185 (Archive Review UI).

tasks.title: UI + Data Engineer: Sync pipeline status to backend so it persists across sessions and survives browser data clearing. Currently `usePipeline.ts` stores pipeline status (discovered/shortlisted/vetted/archived) in localStorage only — it is not written to SQLite and is lost if the browser is cleared. (1) Data Engineer: Add `pipeline_status TEXT` column to the `properties` table (ALTER TABLE or migration-while-copy). Add `PATCH /api/properties/:id/status` endpoint accepting `{ pipeline_status: string }` — validates against `discovered|shortlisted|vetted|archived`, writes to the new column, returns the updated record. (2) Frontend: Update `usePipeline.ts` so `setStatus()` calls `PATCH /api/properties/:id/status` AND updates localStorage as a fallback. On app load, hydrate localStorage from the backend API (GET /api/properties returns pipeline_status per record). This ensures pipeline decisions persist server-side and survive browser resets. (3) Update the analyst README (agents/data_analyst/README.md) to remove the 'archived records' section — analyst no longer sets archived; that column is now exclusively for the user's pipeline decisions. The analyst uses `analyst_flag` and `analyst_notes` instead. (4) Update REQUIREMENTS.md section 4 to reflect that pipeline state is now backend-persisted.
notes: Root cause: usePipeline.ts only writes to localStorage — no backend persistence.
Separation of concerns: pipeline_status (user) is distinct from analyst_flag (analyst). analyst_flag replaces the analyst's use of archived=1.
ADR-014 (market_status) is separate from pipeline_status — both are backend-persisted columns.

2026-04-02: DE portion complete — FE-186 (1): pipeline_status column added via safe migration; PATCH /api/properties/:id/status endpoint implemented with full validation; sync_data.js INSERT statement corrected (pre-existing 44-col/41-placeholder bug fixed) and extended with pipeline_status; analyst README and REQUIREMENTS.md updated to reflect new three-axis model (pipeline_status/user, analyst_flag/analyst, market_status/market).

tasks.title: Bug: SVG NaN rendering — CapitalAppreciationChart and SparklineChart produce invalid path/circle attributes
notes: Root cause chain: extractValue() returns null for missing data → null arithmetic produces NaN → NaN passed to SVG d/cy attributes
Bug 1 — useMacroData.ts hpi_forecasts normalization (line 61-67):
extractValue(f.forecast_12m) returns null when forecast_12m is undefined/missing →
delta: null - 1.2 = NaN → hpi_forecasts array contains NaN values
React error: 'hpi_forecasts.map' type error (already QA-164, partially addressed but NaN propagates)
Bug 2 — SwapRateSignal.tsx renderSparkline (lines 52-75):
sparkline2yr/sparkline5yr arrays may contain NaN from extractValue failures
Math.min/max on array with NaN → NaN → range = NaN → y = NaN
SVG polyline points='x,NaN x,NaN...' → browser console error: 'attribute d'
Fix: filter NaN from points array OR check isNaN before computing coords
const validPoints = points.filter(p => !isNaN(p));
const min = Math.min(...validPoints); // also guard validPoints.length > 0
Bug 3 — CapitalAppreciationChart.tsx allValues (lines 80-94):
yearlyData.flatMap([...]) with empty scenarios array → allValues = []
Math.min/max on empty spread → Infinity/-Infinity → range = NaN
getY(NaN) → NaN → fanPath='M NaN,NaN L...'
Fix: guard yearlyData length AND guard allValues before Math.min/max
if (!allValues.length) return null; // before SVG render
Bug 4 — CapitalAppreciationChart.tsx MC bands (lines 246-277):
mc.yearlyBandP10[0] accessed without length check → undefined → getY(undefined) → NaN
mc.yearlyBandP90 accessed without length check → same issue
Fix: add mc.yearlyBandP10.length check before using [0] OR guard getY with isNaN
const safeGetY = (v: number) => isNaN(v) || !isFinite(v) ? H - pad : getY(v);
Bug 5 — SwapRateSignal line 54-55:
Math.min(...points) / Math.max(...points) with NaN in array → NaN
Fix: same as Bug 2 — filter valid numbers first
Note: SparklineChart.tsx component file exists but is NEVER imported anywhere — dead code.
The real sparklines are in SwapRateSignal.tsx inline renderSparkline function.
Impact: cosmetic — browser console errors but chart renders 'nothing' rather than crashing.
Severity: Low (cosmetic) but violates Bloomberg Terminal quality standard (no console errors in prod).
Priority: High per task — FE should fix to eliminate all console errors from institutional UI.

2026-04-02: Fixed all 4 NaN SVG bugs. Bug 2: SwapRateSignal renderSparkline now filters NaN/Infinity from points before Math.min/max. Bug 3: CapitalAppreciationChart allValues now filters NaN/Infinity and guards empty array before Math.min/max. Bug 4: Added safeGetY() helper that returns H-pad (bottom) for NaN/Infinity/undefined values — used throughout MC band SVG rendering.

tasks.title: Metric Clarity Audit: MarketConditionsBar — all 7 market metrics lack institutional tooltips
notes: REQUIREMENTS.md §8: Every complex metric must have institutional tooltip with definition + methodology + strategic value.
METRIC_DEFINITIONS.md is the authoritative source for all tooltip text.
File to fix: frontend/src/components/MarketConditionsBar.tsx
Current state: ZERO tooltips — no Tooltip component imported or used.
Strategy: Wrap each metric segment in <Tooltip content={...} methodology={...}>
Required tooltip text (from METRIC_DEFINITIONS.md):
1. Market Mode / MOS:
content: 'Market Mode is derived from Months of Supply (MOS). MOS < 4 = Seller's Market (low leverage for buyers).'
methodology: 'Active Listings / Avg. Monthly Sales. < 4m = Seller's Market, 4-6m = Balanced, > 6m = Buyer's Market.'
Wrap around: lines 71-81 (marketMode badge + MOS text)
2. Negotiation Room:
content: 'Negotiation Room is the average discount from asking price in the current quarter.'
methodology: 'Average difference between Asking Price and Sold Price. Based on Land Registry sold data vs. portal asking price.'
Wrap around: lines 85-90
3. Rate Signal (rising/falling/holding):
content: 'Rate Signal reflects the trend in 5yr GBP Swap Rates — a leading indicator for retail mortgage pricing.'
methodology: 'Swap rates sourced from market feeds. Rising = mortgage costs increasing. Falling = mortgage costs decreasing.'
Wrap around: lines 94-99
4. Seasonal Window (score 0-10):
content: 'Seasonal Buy Score (0-10) indicates the optimality of the current window for acquisition.'
methodology: 'Based on historical price dips (Dec/Jan) and inventory surges (Spring). Higher = better buy window.'
Wrap around: lines 103-111
5. Buyer Favour Score (composite 0-10):
content: 'Buyer Favour Score is a composite indicator of acquisition conditions.'
methodology: 'Composite of MOS, Negotiation Room, and Seasonal Score. >7 = Favourable, 4-7 = Neutral, <4 = Unfavourable.'
NOTE: The formula (lines 32-39) should also be documented in tooltip.
Wrap around: lines 115-125
6. MPC Date:
content: 'MPC is the Bank of England Monetary Policy Committee — sets the base rate that governs retail mortgage floors.'
methodology: 'Scheduled MPC meeting dates. Next meeting date sourced from BoE MPC calendar.'
Wrap around: lines 129-133
7. SDLT Badge:
content: 'SDLT (Stamp Duty Land Tax) surcharge tier status.'
methodology: 'Q2 2026 milestone thresholds. Shows days remaining in current SDLT regime.'
Wrap around: lines 135-144
Implementation pattern (see MarketPulse.tsx for working example):
import Tooltip from './Tooltip';
<Tooltip content='...' methodology='...'>
...
</Tooltip>
2026-04-02: All 7 market metrics now wrapped in Tooltip component with institutional definitions and calculation methodology from METRIC_DEFINITIONS.md.

tasks.title: FE (Improvement): Server-side URL dedup check with structured feedback when a submitted URL already exists
notes: SPEC (Product Owner, 2026-04-04):

REQUIREMENT: When a user submits a URL via the Quick-Add box in the Inbox UI, check if that URL already exists in (a) data/inbox/ files or (b) the properties DB. If it exists, return a 409 and show the user a specific message.
SERVER SIDE (Data Engineer — server/index.js POST /api/inbox):
1. Before writing a new RAW file, read all existing data/inbox/*.json files (exclude _processed files)
2. Check the url, urls[], and links[] fields for a match against the submitted URL
3. Also query propSearch.db properties.links JSON field across all rows — handle both string URLs and {url: string} objects
4. If found in inbox: return HTTP 409 with body { found_in: "inbox", filename, message: "URL already in inbox — pending triage" }
5. If found in DB: return HTTP 409 with body { found_in: "db", property_id, address, message: "Already in your property database — {address}" }
6. DB check is non-fatal — if DB is unavailable, proceed with the save and return 201
7. Only write the RAW file if neither check finds a match
FRONTEND SIDE (Frontend Engineer — frontend/src/pages/Inbox.tsx handleQuickAdd):
1. After the POST to /api/inbox, check res.status specifically for 409
2. On 409 with found_in=inbox: setToast({ msg: "Already in inbox — pending triage", type: "info" })
3. On 409 with found_in=db: setToast({ msg: "Already in your database — {address}", type: "info" })
4. On 409: do NOT save to localStorage, do NOT call fetchInbox() — just show the toast and return
5. On 201: proceed with existing success path (saveSubmission, fetchInbox, success toast)
6. The existing FE-213 client-side localStorage guard runs BEFORE the fetch and is the first-pass check
CONTEXT: This complements FE-213 (client-side localStorage check). The server-side check is the authoritative dedup that catches URLs already processed or approved in the past.
acceptance_criteria: Submitting a URL already in data/inbox/ shows "Already in inbox — pending triage" toast
Submitting a URL already in properties DB shows "Already in your database — {address}" toast
Duplicate URL is not written to data/inbox/ in either case
Server returns 409 (not 201) for duplicates — no file is written
All three states (new / inbox-dup / db-dup) produce visually distinct user feedback

tasks.title: Data Engineer (Bug): sync_data.js crash — 47 values for 45 columns in INSERT — DE-165/DE-164 schema regression
notes: Root cause: DE-165 added market_status, last_checked, pipeline_status to the INSERT column list but VALUES only had 47 placeholders (the correct count should match 45 columns). Additionally, DE-164 left the upsert/merge updateStmt as a separate template in the same backtick-quoted SQL string, causing better-sqlite3 to parse only the INSERT statement up to its closing backtick. The VALUES showed 47 ? for 45 columns because the three hardcoded run() values ('active', today, 'discovered') corresponding to the three new columns were passed but the base schema in CREATE TABLE didn't include them (they were only in the server's missingColumns ALTER TABLE).

Fix: Verified that market_status, last_checked, pipeline_status exist in the DB (added by server ALTER TABLE on startup). Restored sync_data.js to original state, then: (1) Verified INSERT has 45 columns (all base fields including market_status/last_checked/pipeline_status); (2) Set VALUES to 45 placeholders; (3) Confirmed insertStmt.run() passes 45 values: 42 newItem properties + 'active' (market_status) + today (last_checked) + 'discovered' (pipeline_status).
The 10 enrichment columns (ltv_match_score, appr_*, rental_yield_*, bedrooms) are set by the enrichment pipeline and frontend, not by sync_data.js.
Also verified: server/index.js startup is clean with full schema init.

tasks.title: UX: /rates page — add Market Verdict hero + affordability signal above all charts.
notes: PROBLEM: The /rates page has 6+ components of equal visual weight with no narrative synthesis. Users see raw numbers without understanding what they mean for their acquisition decision.
INTENT: Add a high-impact Market Verdict hero section at the top of /rates — the first thing users see. Synthesises all rate/market data into one actionable headline.
LAYOUT:
│  BUYERS WINDOW  │  MOS 7.2mo  │  Rate: Falling  │  -£370/mo │
│  BoE cuts underway. Studio/1-Bed in NW3 leads appreciation.│
COMPONENTS:
1. Verdict badge: BUYERS WINDOW / NEUTRAL / SELLERS MARKET — colour coded green/amber/red
2. 4-metric row: MOS | Rate Signal | Buyer Favour | HPI Momentum — all from live data
3. Affordability delta: monthly savings vs peak rates — computed from useAffordability hook
4. Narrative strip: 1-2 sentence summary pulled from SwapRateSignal + HPI + Area data
DATA SOURCES (all from existing hooks):
- MOS: useMacroData → market_conditions.mos
- Rate Signal: useMacroData → swap_rates trend
- Buyer Favour: useMacroData → market_conditions.buyer_favour_score
- Affordability delta: useAffordability — peak rate vs current rate for £600K mortgage
- Narrative: computed from area_heat_index + HPI momentum
VERDICT LOGIC:
BUYERS WINDOW (green): MOS > 6 AND rate trend falling AND buyer_favour > 6
NEUTRAL (amber): MOS 4-6 OR rate trend flat
SELLERS MARKET (red): MOS < 4 AND rate trend rising
RESPONSIVE:
Desktop: horizontal 4-col grid + narrative strip
Tablet: 2-col grid + narrative
Mobile: stacked cards, verdict badge + 2 metrics per row
FILE: RatesPage.tsx — add as first child of max-w-7xl container.
ACCEPTANCE: Users understand the macro context in <5 seconds. Affordability delta is actionable. Narrative updates reactively.

tasks.title: UX: Install @visx/voronoi and add pixel-precise hover to all line charts.
notes: Install: npm install @visx/voronoi
@visx/voronoi computes a Voronoi tessellation from data points — every pixel is assigned to its nearest data point.
Use VoronoiMesh as invisible hover targets on HPIHistoryChart, LondonPrimePremiumChart, PropertyPriceEvolution.
CURRENT: Charts use a full-width invisible rect — works but no precision.
VORONOI: hover anywhere near a data point and it snaps to the correct point. Much more reliable UX.
Acceptance: Line chart hover is precise — no need to click exactly on the line to see tooltip.

tasks.title: Fix Purchasing Power Index tooltip positioning
tasks.description: The Purchasing Power Index chart tooltip does not fill the full box and appears in the wrong position when hovering. Issue is in PurchasingPowerChart.tsx where the SVG uses viewBox with preserveAspectRatio but tooltip coordinates are calculated in SVG coordinate space while being applied in screen space. Fix needed: (1) Calculate the actual rendered position by accounting for the SVG's scaling ratio, OR (2) Use a container-based positioning approach that reads the actual rendered bounding box, OR (3) Use useTooltipInPortal with proper scaling to ensure tooltip appears at the correct screen position relative to the hovered point.
tasks.notes: Tooltip appears disconnected from the chart due to viewBox scaling mismatch.

tasks.title: Fix London HPI Historical Trajectory tooltip positioning
tasks.description: The London HPI Historical Trajectory chart tooltip does not fill the full box and appears in the wrong position when hovering. Same root cause as VISX-017 (Purchasing Power Index). In HPIHistoryChart.tsx: The SVG uses viewBox="0 0 800 300" with preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet", but tooltip coordinates (tooltipLeft, tooltipTop) passed to TooltipWithBounds are in SVG viewBox coordinate space while being applied as screen coordinates. Fix needed: Convert SVG viewBox coordinates to actual rendered screen coordinates before passing to the tooltip — either via getBoundingClientRect() on the SVG container, useTooltipInPortal with proper SVG scaling, or calculating the actual rendered aspect ratio and offset.
tasks.notes: Same viewBox scaling mismatch issue as VISX-017 (Purchasing Power Index). Fix approach should be consistent across both charts — consider extracting shared tooltip positioning logic into a reusable hook.

tasks.title: Fix London HPI Historical Trajectory axis label font sizes
tasks.description: The London HPI Historical Trajectory chart axis labels (x-axis year labels and y-axis HPI index values) are too small to read. Root cause: HPIHistoryChart.tsx uses viewBox="0 0 800 300" with preserveAspectRatio, and all SVG text elements use viewBox-space fontSize values (2.5px, 2.8px) that scale down to near-invisible on screen. Fix: Either (1) switch from viewBox-based SVG to a container-width responsive SVG approach (no viewBox, use percentage-based positioning) so font sizes are in screen pixels, OR (2) use a larger viewBox with correspondingly larger fontSize values that still read well at target display size, OR (3) use getBoundingClientRect() to detect the rendered SVG dimensions and scale font sizes accordingly. The axis labels (year markers on x-axis, HPI index values on y-axis) and annotation event labels must be clearly legible at a glance.
tasks.notes: User explicitly flagged axis labels as unreadable. This compounds the tooltip positioning issue (VISX-018) — both stem from the viewBox scaling approach.

tasks.title: Improve Area × Metric Heatmap UX clarity and fix tooltip positioning
tasks.description:
Two issues with the AreaMetricHeatmap component on the Market page:

1. POOR UX / CLARITY: The heatmap is hard to understand. Issues: (a) Metric column labels are truncated to first word only ("Heat" not "Heat Index", "Rental" not "Rental Yield") — loses meaning; (b) No explanation of what each metric means or how values are derived; (c) The color legend (red→green gradient) is not explained — what does "high" vs "low" mean for each metric? (d) No hover state change on cells to show interactivity; (e) Tooltip is the only way to see actual values — users cannot scan the grid without hovering every cell.

Fix: (a) Show full metric names as column headers above the heatmap grid (not rotated/abbreviated); (b) Add a descriptive subtitle row explaining each metric in one short phrase (e.g., "Market velocity 0-10", "Annual appreciation %", "Gross rental yield %", "12-month HPI forecast"); (c) Add a visible hover state (border highlight or brightness change) on cells to signal interactivity; (d) Consider showing the numeric value directly inside each cell for key metrics, or at minimum ensure the tooltip appears clearly above the hovered cell.

2. TOOLTIP POSITIONING: Tooltip appears in wrong position. Tooltip is rendered outside the heatmap SVG container (TooltipWithBounds with left/top coordinates) but may be clipped or offset due to parent container stacking context/overflow. Fix: Wrap the tooltip in a portal or ensure it renders at the correct viewport position — consider using @visx/tooltip's useTooltipInPortal, or ensure the tooltip container has proper z-index and overflow handling.
tasks.notes: User flagged: (1) hard to understand how it works — needs better labels, metric explanations, and visible hover states; (2) tooltips not positioned correctly. These are separate fixes but share the same component.

tasks.title: FE: Build Seasonal Market Cycle component for Rates & Scenarios page
tasks.description:
Design and build a new component showing the yearly London property market seasonal cycle, displayed on the Rates & Scenarios page.

Concept: A circular/radial cycle diagram (clock-style or ring chart) showing the 12-month market cycle with the following phases:
- Jan-Feb: Post-holiday trough — lowest supply, subdued demand
- Mar-May: Spring surge — highest supply (new listings flood in), peak demand, prices rise fastest
- Jun-Aug: Summer lull — activity slows (holidays), demand moderates
- Sep-Nov: Autumn rush — second busiest period, supply-demand balance, prices hold
- Dec: Christmas slowdown — minimal activity

Visual design (Bloomberg meets Linear):
- Use a circular SVG ring or radar-style chart
- Inner ring = supply (listings volume by month)
- Outer ring = demand (buyer activity by month)
- Colour gradient: cold/dormant (slate) → hot/peak (emerald/orange) → cooling (amber)
- A visible needle/marker showing current month position in the cycle
- Phase labels around the ring: Spring Surge, Summer Lull, Autumn Rush, Winter Trough
- Key stats per phase shown on hover

Data: Use historical inventory/new-listing data from macro_trend.json inventory_velocity and transaction_volumes. Where empirical data is sparse, derive normalised seasonal indices from ONS/BoE transaction volumes (known Jan-Dec patterns). The component should accept month-of-year as input to position the indicator needle.

Placement: Add to RatesPage.tsx between the Swap Rate Signal banner and the HPI Historical Trajectory section.

Acceptance: Circular cycle chart renders on RatesPage. Needle correctly positions at current month. Supply/demand bands visually distinct. Hovering a phase shows key stats.
tasks.notes: Known seasonal pattern: Spring surge (Mar-May) = peak prices, Autumn rush (Sep-Nov) = second busiest. Implementation approach: SVG ring chart using @visx/shape Arc or custom polar chart. Data from inventory_velocity + transaction_volumes in macro_trend.json.

tasks.title: DE: Surface seasonal supply/demand indices in macro_trend.json
tasks.description:
The Seasonal Market Cycle component (VISX-021) needs empirical seasonal indices for London property market supply (new listings) and demand (transactions/buyer activity) by month. Currently no dedicated seasonal cycle data exists in macro_trend.json.

Source and compute seasonal indices:
1. inventory_velocity: already has monthly supply signals — extract normalise to a 12-month seasonal index (Jan=low, Mar-May=high, Jul-Aug=mid/summer-dip, Sep-Nov=autumn-peak, Dec=low)
2. transaction_volumes: ONS Land Registry monthly transaction counts for London — derive a normalised seasonal index from 5yr+ monthly averages
3. Create two new top-level keys in macro_trend.json:
   - seasonal_supply_index: array of 12 values [jan..dec], normalised 0-10
   - seasonal_demand_index: array of 12 values [jan..dec], normalised 0-10
4. Add a derived net_balance index: demand - supply per month (positive = seller's market, negative = buyer's market)

The indices should reflect the known London market pattern: Spring (Mar-May) is seller's market (high demand meets high supply), Winter (Dec-Feb) is quieter buyer's market, Autumn (Sep-Nov) is balanced/moderate.

Write the normalised indices to the SQLite global_context macro_trend record so they're available via the API to the frontend component.
tasks.notes: User wants to see where we are in the yearly cycle. This DE task computes the empirical seasonal indices needed by the FE component. The FE component can use fallback hardcoded values if this DE task is not yet done.

Result: sidebar showed £222K deposit when user had fixed 15% (£75K) configured. Fixed by branching on depositMode: fixed uses depositPct, auto uses budget-derivation. Also fixes monthlyPayment and stressTest to be consistent with selected mode.

tasks.title: Sync shortlist/other pipeline actions to backend — eliminate localStorage-only persistence
notes: PROBLEM: usePipeline.ts only writes to localStorage. Status changes survive browser clear but do NOT persist to the SQLite backend.
Current behavior: user shortlists a property — written to localStorage only — not in /api/properties response — not in PATCH payload for backend.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

2. localStorage remains as fallback (for offline/degraded server scenarios)
3. On app mount: hydrate pipeline state from GET /api/properties response (pipeline_status field) before rendering
4. Optimistic update: update local state immediately, rollback on API failure
5. Server 500 errors surface a toast notification — do not silently swallow failures
6. No duplicate PATCH calls (debounce rapid status changes within 300ms)
TECHNICAL NOTES:
- Server endpoint PATCH /api/properties/:id/status already exists (server/index.js:431+)
- Server writes pipeline_status to SQLite properties.pipeline_status column
- GET /api/properties already returns pipeline_status for each property

- The fix should be in usePipeline.ts — the hook is the correct place for write + sync logic

REFERENCE: FE-186 implemented server-side; this task implements client-side sync to match.

tasks.title: UX: Property ranking — enable users to assign custom priority order within shortlisted properties
notes: PROBLEM: Users cannot assign their own priority order to properties. The system sorts by Alpha Score, Value Gap, etc., but provides no way to express personal preference.
UX RESEARCH: Proposed patterns — user to confirm before implementation:
PATTERN A — Drag-and-drop reorder in Table/Grid view (recommended)
- User can drag rows/cards to reorder within a status filter
- Visual: numbered rank badge on each row (1, 2, 3...) or grip handle
- Persisted server-side (new column property_rank)
- Sort option: My Priority — shows user-defined order first
PATTERN B — Star rating / tier system
- 1-5 stars or Hot/Warm/Cool tiers assignable per property
- Simple click interaction (less friction than drag)
- Persisted server-side
- Sort by stars/tier within each pipeline status
PATTERN C — Comparison ranking
- Pairwise comparison: A vs B — which do you prefer?
- ELO-style algorithm derives ranking from comparisons
- More engaging but higher implementation complexity
RECOMMENDATION: Start with Pattern A (drag-and-drop) for Table/Grid views within each status filter, with a user_rank column. Pattern B can be added as a secondary layer.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (Pattern A):
1. Shortlisted properties in Table view show a drag handle or rank badge
2. User can drag properties to reorder within Shortlisted (and optionally within all statuses)
3. Property rank is persisted server-side (new property_rank INTEGER column)
4. Filter/sort panel includes My Priority as a sort option
5. Rank persists across sessions and is hydrated from backend on mount
SERVER CHANGES:
- New column: property_rank INTEGER in properties table
- GET /api/properties: ORDER BY property_rank ASC when sortBy=user_priority
- New endpoint: PATCH /api/properties/:id/rank { rank: number }
DATA ENGINEER: Add property_rank column and update GET /api/properties sort handling.

tasks.title: HPIHistoryChart: x-axis gap — historical line stops short of right chart edge
notes: Component: HPIHistoryChart.tsx (frontend/src/components/)

Bug: The historical line (green) stops at the last history data point (index 59 of 60 months) but the xScale domain extends to xMax=95. The line only reaches ~62% of the chart width and there is a visible gap to the right edge.
Root cause: xScale domain is [0, xMax] where xMax=displayHistory.length+36-1=95, but historyLinePath data only has x values 0-59. The LinePath has no extra points to extend to xMax.

Fix: Add a SVG clipPath that clips everything to [LEFT_PAD, W-PAD] x [PAD, H-PAD]. Add <clipPath id="chart-area"><rect x={LEFT_PAD} y={PAD} width={W-PAD-LEFT_PAD} height={H-PAD-PAD}/></clipPath> and apply clipPath="url(#chart-area)" to the SVG group. This ensures all lines are visually clamped to the chart boundary without changing data geometry.
Acceptance criteria:
1. Historical HPI line (green) visibly reaches the right edge of the chart area
2. Projection fan area (blue) is clipped to the chart boundary
3. No visual gaps on the right side of the chart at any viewport (375/768/1280/1920px)
4. Existing 11 HPI chart tests continue to pass
Implemented. Build clean. Smoke tests pass.

tasks.title: HPIHistoryChart: tooltip appears offset from mouse cursor (misaligned horizontally and vertically)
notes: Component: HPIHistoryChart.tsx (frontend/src/components/)
Bug: When hovering over data points on the HPI chart, the tooltip appears visibly offset from the cursor — shifted right and down.

Root cause: tooltipLeft/tooltipTop come from getX()/getY() which return SVG pixel coordinates. TooltipWithBounds renders as position:absolute inside the SVG parent div which has className="px-4 py-3 relative" (16px horizontal padding, 12px vertical padding). The SVG element fills this div starting at its content box. SVG coords 0,0 = SVG top-left, which is at 16px from the parent div's left edge. Tooltip is positioned at left=tooltipLeft, top=tooltipTop relative to the parent div — so it's offset by the div's padding.

Fix: Subtract the parent div padding from the tooltip coordinates: left={tooltipLeft - 16} top={tooltipTop - 12}. This converts SVG screen coords to parent-relative coords that match where the mouse event originated.

Alternative diagnosis: If the SVG has overflow:visible and the chart content is clamped by a clipPath (FE-223), ensure the hover zone polygon coordinates are also relative to the SVG coordinate space and that tooltip coords are correspondingly offset.
Acceptance criteria:
1. Tooltip appears directly at the cursor when hovering a data point
2. Tooltip does not overflow outside the chart container on mobile (375px viewport)
3. Tooltip still shows date and index values correctly
4. Add Playwright test: hover over chart center and verify tooltip is visible and contains date/index text
Implemented. Build clean. Smoke tests pass.

tasks.title: SeasonalMarketCycle: tooltip appears at wrong position (offset right/down from cursor)
notes: Component: SeasonalMarketCycle.tsx (frontend/src/components/)
Page: /rates — Seasonal Market Cycle section
Bug: Tooltip appears visibly offset to the right and below the cursor when hovering over any month segment.

Root cause: handleMouseMove computes tooltipLeft = clientX - rect.left + 12 and tooltipTop = clientY - rect.top - 10. TooltipWithBounds from @visx/tooltip renders with position:fixed by default (viewport-relative). clientX and clientX are viewport coords; rect.left/rect.top are also viewport coords. Subtracting rect.left from clientX gives element-relative coords, but position:fixed then re-interprets these as viewport coords — effectively double-subtracting rect.left, placing the tooltip 130px too far left (equal to viewBox cx offset) plus the +12 offset.

Fix: Pass pure viewport coordinates to the tooltip — left={clientX + 12} top={clientY - 10}. No rect subtraction needed. This is the correct pattern for position:fixed tooltips. Remove the getBoundingClientRect call entirely from handleMouseMove.
Implementation change:
const handleMouseMove = useCallback((monthIdx: number, clientX: number, clientY: number) => {
... remove svgEl param and getBoundingClientRect call
showTooltip({ tooltipLeft: clientX + 12, tooltipTop: clientY - 10, ... })
Also update onMouseMove calls in supply/demand segments to pass (seg.i, e.clientX, e.clientY) directly.
Also add style={{position:'absolute'}} to TooltipWithBounds to make it layer above the SVG cleanly.
Acceptance criteria:
1. Tooltip appears immediately adjacent to cursor on hover over any month segment
2. Tooltip does not overflow off-screen on mobile (375px) viewport
3. Tooltip still shows month name, phase, supply/demand scores, and phase description
4. Add Playwright test: hover over the chart and verify tooltip position is within 40px of mouse
Implemented. Build clean. Smoke tests pass.

tasks.title: SeasonalMarketCycle: chart is too small — use ParentSize for responsive pixel-accurate sizing
notes: Component: SeasonalMarketCycle.tsx (frontend/src/components/)
Page: /rates — Seasonal Market Cycle section

Bug: The circular market cycle chart is hardcoded to height=240px and width=100% with a viewBox that maps to 260x260 units. On mobile (375px) the chart is tiny and unreadable. On desktop it is undersized relative to the available card space.

Root cause: The SVG uses width="100%" and a fixed height prop (default 240px). The viewBox is 260x260 with a center of (0,0). This creates a circular chart with max radius 130px = 260px diameter. With height=240px and width=240px (on mobile) the chart barely fits. The inner/outer ring radii (58px/88px) are hardcoded in viewBox units, not responsive to actual container size.
Fix: Migrate to ParentSize pattern (same as HPIHistoryChart.tsx which already handles this correctly):
1. Import ParentSize from @visx/responsive
2. Wrap the SVG in <ParentSize> to get actual pixel width/height
3. Set SVG width/height to the pixel values from ParentSize
4. Calculate cx, cy, and ring radii in PIXEL coordinates (not viewBox coords)
5. Remove viewBox attribute — no longer needed
6. Scale the month label radius: labelR = Math.min(W, H) * 0.38
7. Scale INNER_R = Math.min(W, H) * 0.22, OUTER_R = Math.min(W, H) * 0.34
8. Scale needleLen = INNER_R - 6
9. Update polarToCartesian to use cx=W/2, cy=H/2 instead of (0,0)
10. Scale month font sizes: fontSize = Math.min(W, H) * 0.035
11. Keep viewBox approach as fallback if ParentSize causes issues: viewBox={`0 0 ${maxDim} ${maxDim}`} where maxDim = Math.max(W, H)
RatesPage.tsx change: Remove the w-[240px] constraint from the SeasonalMarketCycle container div (line 85: <div className="flex-shrink-0 w-[240px]">). Allow it to fill available space.
Acceptance criteria:
1. Chart scales to fill available card width on all viewports (375/768/1280/1920px)
2. Chart maintains 1:1 aspect ratio (always circular, never squashed)
3. Ring radii and label sizes scale proportionally
4. No horizontal overflow on mobile
5. Chart remains centered in its card area
Implemented. Build clean. Smoke tests pass.

tasks.title: Affordability: monthly payment display does not match configured budget — getBudgetProfile auto-mode logic is incorrect
notes: Hook: useAffordability.ts — getBudgetProfile() function (lines 385-439)
Components affected: AffordabilityNode.tsx (monthlyPayment display), AffordabilitySettings.tsx (Payment Scenarios section, stress test)

BUG — In auto deposit mode, monthlyPayment always equals the configured monthlyBudget regardless of property price. This makes the display misleading: for properties well within budget, the user sees their full budget as the payment when the true mortgage payment is significantly lower.
Example: monthlyBudget=£6,000, termYears=25, mortgageRate=4.55%
maxMortgage = calculateMortgageFromPayment(6000, 4.55%, 25) = £955,193
For £500K property: deposit=£0, mortgageAmount=£955K (capped), ltvBand=90%
actualMonthly (line 418) = monthlyBudget = £6,000 [WRONG]
Correct payment for £500K at 4.55% over 25yr = £5,251
Discrepancy: £749/month (12.5% below configured budget)

Root cause (line 401-402): In auto mode, mortgageAmount = maxMortgage (the budget-implied ceiling), not the actual property price. Then actualMonthly = monthlyBudget (direct pass-through). This means the displayed payment = budget regardless of what the mortgage for THIS property actually costs.
Fix — In auto mode, monthlyPayment should be the actual calculated payment for the property's mortgage at the LTV band's rate:
const actualMonthly = depositMode === 'fixed'
? calculateMortgageFromPayment(mortgageAmount, adjustedRate)
: calculateMortgageFromPayment(Math.min(mortgageAmount, propertyPrice), adjustedRate)
// In auto mode: use min(propertyPrice, maxMortgage) as the mortgage principal
// This correctly represents what the user would actually pay for THIS specific property
// NOTE: The stress test in auto mode (line 424) also needs updating to use Math.min(mortgageAmount, propertyPrice)
Additional fix — Stress test calculation (line 422-424):
In auto mode, stressMonthly = calculateMortgageFromPayment(maxMortgage, stressRate)
But maxMortgage is the BUDGET-implied ceiling (£955K), not the property's actual mortgage.
For a £500K property, stressMonthly would be calculated on £955K, not £500K.
Fix: use Math.min(mortgageAmount, propertyPrice) for stressMonthly in auto mode too.
UI note: After this fix, for Entry Level (£500K) with £6,000 budget, monthlyPayment will show £5,251.
The 'Max Affordable' card (affordableRange.max) and the scenario payment will now be consistent.
Acceptance criteria:
1. For a £500K property with £6,000/month budget, monthlyPayment displays ~£5,251 (not £6,000)
2. For a £1.5M property with £6,000/month budget, monthlyPayment displays ~£5,006 (not £6,000)
3. Stress test monthly payment is calculated on the property's actual mortgage, not the budget ceiling
4. The 'Max Affordable' card and scenario payments are numerically consistent
5. In fixed deposit mode, behavior is unchanged (monthlyPayment = calculated from mortgageAmount)
Implemented. Build clean. Smoke tests pass.

tasks.title: AffordabilitySettings LTV Match Analysis: mortgage amount calculations apply LTV fraction twice
notes: Component: AffordabilitySettings.tsx — LTV Match Analysis section (lines 599-613)
Hook: useAffordability.ts
BUG — The mortgage amounts shown for each LTV band (90%/85%/75%) are calculated as affordableRange.max × 0.85 × ltv_fraction.
Since affordableRange.max is already computed at 85% LTV (the user's budget constraint), multiplying by 0.85 again applies the deposit percentage twice.
Current formulas (WRONG):
At 90% LTV: affordableRange.max × 0.85 × 0.90 = max × 0.765
At 85% LTV: affordableRange.max × 0.85 × 0.85 = max × 0.722  [= maxMortgage, coincidentally close]
At 75% LTV: affordableRange.max × 0.85 × 0.75 = max × 0.637
Where affordableRange.max = maxMortgage / 0.85 (from AffordabilitySettings line 61)
maxMortgage = calculateMortgageFromPayment(monthlyBudget) = £955K
affordableRange.max = £955K / 0.85 = £1,123K
Example with maxAffordable = £1,123,757:
Correct formulas:
At 90% LTV: affordableRange.max × 0.90 = £1,011K
At 85% LTV: affordableRange.max × 0.85 = £955K  (≈ maxMortgage — correct as-is)
At 75% LTV: affordableRange.max × 0.75 = £843K
Fix — Change the calculations in AffordabilitySettings.tsx lines 601-611:
'At 90% LTV:':  Change from {affordableRange.max * 0.85 * 0.85 / 1000).toFixed(0)}K
to   {Math.round(affordableRange.max * 0.90 / 1000).toFixed(0)}K
'At 75% LTV:':  Change from {affordableRange.max * 0.85 * 0.75 / 1000).toFixed(0)}K
to   {Math.round(affordableRange.max * 0.75 / 1000).toFixed(0)}K
'At 85% LTV:':  This one is correct (maxMortgage ≈ max * 0.85). Keep as-is.
Acceptance criteria:
1. At 90% LTV row shows ~£1,011K (not £859K)
2. At 75% LTV row shows ~£843K (not £716K)
3. At 85% LTV row unchanged (~£955K = maxMortgage)
4. The three rows are now consistent: 90% > 85% > 75%
Implemented. Build clean. Smoke tests pass.

tasks.title: Tooltip coordinate offset: systematic SVG-container padding mismatch across chart components
notes: Components affected: HPIHistoryChart.tsx (FE-224), SeasonalMarketCycle.tsx (FE-225), PurchasingPowerChart.tsx, LondonPrimePremiumChart.tsx, RentalYieldVsGiltChart.tsx, BoERatePathChart.tsx

SYSTEMIC BUG — Root cause: All chart components use tooltipLeft/tooltipTop in SVG coordinate space (pixels from SVG origin), but TooltipWithBounds renders with position:absolute relative to the nearest positioned ancestor — the SVG's parent div which has className padding (e.g., px-4 py-3).

The SVG origin (0,0) is inset from the parent div's content box by the div's padding values (typically 16px left, 12px top). Tooltip renders at left=tooltipLeft (SVG space), which is 16px too far right from the user's perspective.
Affected files and their container padding:
1. HPIHistoryChart.tsx — parent div: className="px-4 py-3 relative" → offset by (16px, 12px)
2. SeasonalMarketCycle.tsx — parent div: className="relative" (no padding, but uses viewBox centered at 0,0 → offset by cx=130px, cy=130px)
3. PurchasingPowerChart.tsx — parent div: className="px-2 pt-2 pb-4" → offset by (8px, 8px)
4. LondonPrimePremiumChart.tsx — parent div: check className, likely px-4 or similar
5. RentalYieldVsGiltChart.tsx — parent div: check className, likely px-4 or similar
6. BoERatePathChart.tsx — parent div: className="relative" (no padding, tooltip uses MARGIN.left=60px for x, so off by 60px)
GENERIC FIX (apply to all chart components):
After computing tooltipLeft/tooltipTop in SVG pixel space, subtract the SVG container's padding offset:
- Find the nearest positioned ancestor (the SVG's parent div)
- Compute: tooltipLeft = svgCoordX - parentDivPaddingLeft
- Compute: tooltipTop  = svgCoordY - parentDivPaddingTop
ALTERNATIVE FIX (simpler, consistent across all components):
Move the TooltipWithBounds rendering INSIDE the ParentSize callback, right after the SVG closes, using SVG pixel coords directly.
This ensures the tooltip is a sibling of the SVG inside the same ParentSize div, and the offset is consistent.
For components using localPoint(e) which gives SVG-element-relative coords:
localPoint returns coords relative to the SVG element's padding box. These must also be offset by the SVG's parent div's padding to be correct for the tooltip's position:absolute parent.

BoERatePathChart specific fix: tooltipLeft = MARGIN.left + xScale(...) — since SVG starts at div.left (no padding), this value IS the SVG coord. Subtract 0 = keep as-is. But tooltipTop = rel.y - 40 uses localPoint.y which is SVG-relative — should work correctly if SVG has no top padding.
Acceptance criteria:
1. All chart tooltips appear within 20px of the hovered data point on all viewports
2. Tooltip does not overflow off-screen on mobile (375px)
3. Playwright tooltip offset tests added for all affected charts
Implemented. Build clean. Smoke tests pass.

tasks.title: AffordabilitySettings: 'Your Affordable Range' card does not update when monthly budget or loan term changes
notes: Files: useAffordability.ts (hook), AffordabilitySettings.tsx (component)
BUG 1 — termYears missing from calculateMortgageFromPayment dependency array
Location: useAffordability.ts, line 269
Current: useCallback((...) => {...}, [mortgageRate, termYears])
PROBLEM: termYears IS in the function body (used as 'years' parameter default) but NOT in the dependency array.
Impact: When termYears changes (e.g., 25yr → 20yr), React returns a STALE cached function with the old term baked in.
This makes affordableRange stale even when monthlyBudget changes, because affordableRange depends on calculateMortgageFromPayment.
Fix: Add termYears to the dependency array: [mortgageRate, termYears]
BUG 2 — Stale closure risk in affordableRange useMemo (secondary, fix BUG 1 first)
Location: AffordabilitySettings.tsx, lines 56-64
affordableRange depends on calculateMortgageFromPayment as a function reference.
If calculateMortgageFromPayment is stale (BUG 1), affordableRange is stale.
Additionally: termYears is used in the mortgage calculation (via calculateMortgageFromPayment) but is not
listed as a direct dependency of affordableRange, making it an implicit dependency.
Fix: Either (a) fix BUG 1, OR (b) add termYears to affordableRange's dependency array:
[monthlyBudget, depositMode, depositPct, calculateMortgageFromPayment, termYears]
Better fix: Inline the mortgage calculation in affordableRange to break the stale-closure chain:
RECOMMENDED FIX — Inline mortgage calc in affordableRange (most robust):
const affordableRange = useMemo(() => {
const monthlyRate = mortgageRate / 100 / 12;
const numPayments = termYears * 12;
const pvFactor = (1 - Math.pow(1 + monthlyRate, -numPayments)) / monthlyRate;
const maxMortgage = monthlyBudget * pvFactor;
const effectiveDepositPct = depositMode === 'fixed' ? depositPct : 15;
const ltvFraction = 1 - effectiveDepositPct / 100;
const max = Math.round(maxMortgage / ltvFraction);
const min = Math.round(max * 0.85);
return { min, max };
}, [monthlyBudget, mortgageRate, termYears, depositMode, depositPct]);
This removes the dependency on calculateMortgageFromPayment entirely and lists all direct inputs explicitly.
Acceptance criteria:
1. 'Your Affordable Range' card updates IMMEDIATELY when monthly budget slider is dragged (onMouseUp)
2. 'Your Affordable Range' card updates IMMEDIATELY when loan term is changed (15yr/20yr/25yr/30yr)
3. 'Your Affordable Range' card is correct on initial page load from localStorage
4. 'Max Affordable' value in BudgetSlider footer also updates (it already uses the same hook monthlyBudget)
5. Playwright test: change budget via preset button and verify affordable range text changes within 500ms
Implemented. Build clean. Smoke tests pass.

tasks.title: DE: Create enrichment_requests table and API endpoints — server-side plumbing for ADR-018
notes: PURPOSE: Implement the server-side infrastructure for user-initiated enrichment requests per ADR-018.
REFERENCE: ADR-018 (DECISIONS.md) — full design specification is documented there.
DEPENDENCY: FE-229 (frontend modal) depends on these endpoints being live.
TASKS:
1. New SQLite table: enrichment_requests

Columns: id (TEXT PK), property_id (TEXT FK -> properties.id), requested_fields (TEXT JSON array), user_notes (TEXT), status (TEXT DEFAULT pending CHECK IN pending|in_progress|completed|failed|cancelled), analyst_notes (TEXT), created_at (TEXT), updated_at (TEXT)
Index: CREATE INDEX idx_enrichment_requests_status ON enrichment_requests(status)
Index: CREATE INDEX idx_enrichment_requests_property ON enrichment_requests(property_id)
2. POST /api/enrichment-requests — create a new enrichment request
Body: { property_id, requested_fields: string[], user_notes?: string }
Guard: return 400 if a pending/in_progress request already exists for this property_id
Guard: return 400 if property_id does not exist in properties table
On success: return 201 { id, status: pending }
3. GET /api/enrichment-requests — list all requests (for analyst dashboard)
Query params: ?status=pending&property_id=xyz (both optional)
Returns full request objects including property address snapshot
4. GET /api/enrichment-requests/:id — get single request
5. PATCH /api/enrichment-requests/:id/status — analyst updates status (in_progress|completed|failed|cancelled)
Body: { status, analyst_notes }
Only analyst should call this (no auth in this system — document in protocol)
On status=completed: analyst_notes should describe what was fetched/updated
6. GET /api/properties/:id/enrichment-request — check if property has an active request
Returns { has_pending_request: bool, request_id?, status? }
Used by FE to show/hide the request button and badge
7. Property last_checked update: when analyst completes a request, UPDATE properties SET last_checked = NOW()
SERVER FILE: server/index.js

tasks.title: DAT: enrichment_requests polling workflow follow-on [DAT-189]
notes: PURPOSE: Formalise the Data Analyst workflow for processing user-initiated enrichment requests from the queue.
REFERENCE: ADR-018 analyst workflow (DECISIONS.md) — already documented.
WORKFLOW:
At the start of every analyst session:
1. GET /api/enrichment-requests?status=pending
2. For each pending request:
a. PATCH /api/enrichment-requests/:id/status with { status: in_progress }
b. Review the requested_fields (e.g., sqft, EPC, images, tenure)
c. Source the missing data from: agent websites, UK EPC Register, Rightmove/Zoopla deep scrape
d. UPDATE the properties table with the fetched fields
e. PATCH /api/enrichment-requests/:id/status with { status: completed, analyst_notes: "..." }
3. Process max 3 requests in parallel (avoid overloading scraping targets)
DOCUMENTATION:
- Update agents/data_analyst/README.md with a new section: "Enrichment Request Workflow"
- Document the polling command: jq .tasks[] | select(.status=="Todo") tasks/tasks.json  (for task tracking)
- Document the API calls above
- Add to LEARN.md: analyst must check /api/enrichment-requests on every session start
CONFLICT RULES:
- Do not create duplicate pending requests for the same property
- If a property is archived, the analyst should PATCH the request as failed with analyst_notes: "Property archived"
- If enrichment fails after 3 attempts, PATCH as failed with descriptive analyst_notes
tasks.resolution: Enrichment Request Workflow section added to agents/data_analyst/README.md. Protocol 06_ENRICHMENT_QUEUE.md already existed with full field-by-field sourcing methods. LEARN.md entry (2026-04-07) already documented the session-start requirement. Queue verified empty on this session launch.

tasks.title: Fix PropertyDetail monthly payment display — localStorage corruption guard and demo data defaults
tasks.description:
User reported seeing "Affordability Configure Monthly Payment £117,197,086" on the property page.

Root causes identified:
1. useAffordability.ts useState initializers read localStorage directly WITHOUT clamping — a corrupted
   propSearch_ltv_budget value (e.g. from a test or accidental edit) would persist until the next
   user interaction and could produce astronomically large mortgage/payment values on the property page.
2. demo_master.json has ground_rent=undefined for all 69 properties and service_charge=undefined for 40/69.
   calculateMonthlyOutlay does property.service_charge/12 and property.ground_rent/12 — undefined/12=NaN
   propagates into the monthly outlay display.

Fixes applied:
1. Clamp all useState initializers (monthlyBudget, termYears, depositPct) to their valid ranges on init.
2. Add service_charge (£4,000/yr default) and ground_rent (£250/yr default) to PropertyContext normalization.

Note: QA was unable to reproduce £117,197,086 from any combination of valid data inputs. The most plausible
cause is corrupted localStorage state. This defensive fix prevents any future corruption from surfacing.
notes: PURPOSE: Allow users to manually mark a property as "re-checked" from the /property/:id page. The action is asynchronous — the UX must reflect this so the user is never left wondering if it worked.
REFERENCE: ArchiveReview.tsx already has a working handleRecheck() implementation — mirror that pattern.
REFERENCE: server/index.js:405 — POST /api/properties/:id/check already exists. Updates last_checked to today.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. PropertyDetail page shows a "Re-check" or "Mark Verified" action button near the PipelineTracker
2. Button click triggers POST /api/properties/:id/check — action is async, page does NOT reload
3. Optimistic UI update: on click, last_checked freshness badge immediately turns green (CURRENT) before server responds
4. Loading state: button shows a spinner and is disabled while request is in-flight
5. Success: green toast notification "Property re-checked — verified today" (after server confirms)
6. Error: red toast "Re-check failed — please try again" + freshness badge reverts to previous state
7. Debounce: prevent duplicate calls within 300ms
8. last_checked date displayed on page (e.g., "Last verified: 2026-04-07")
ASYNC UX REQUIREMENTS:
- The page must NOT navigate or refresh — this is an inline async action
- Freshness badge updates optimistically on click (green/CURRENT), with rollback on error
- Toast notifications provide confirmation without requiring the user to look for a changed number
- Button returns to idle state after completion or error — no stuck loading states
IMPLEMENTATION: Use the same handleRecheck pattern from ArchiveReview.tsx (lines 145-163) as reference. Add isRechecking state, recheckMsg state. Refresh property data in context after success.
UI PLACEMENT: Inside or adjacent to PipelineTracker section. Style as secondary/ghost button — do not compete with pipeline actions.

tasks.title: FE: Async UX patterns for Recheck and Enrichment Request — optimistic updates, toasts, debounce
notes: PURPOSE: Both the Recheck button (FE-231) and the Enrichment Request modal (FE-229) are async backend operations. This task defines the shared async UX pattern they must both follow so the experience is consistent and the user always knows what is happening.
SHARED ASYNC UX PATTERN:
1. OPTIMISTIC UPDATE — apply result immediately in UI before server confirms
- Recheck: freshness badge turns green (CURRENT) on click, before POST returns
- Enrichment request: amber "Pending Enrichment" badge appears immediately on submit
- Avoid: showing old state, showing a loading-only state with no visual change
2. LOADING STATE — button is disabled + spinner while request is in-flight
- Button text changes to "Checking..." or similar
- Prevent duplicate submissions via disabled prop
- Debounce: 300ms guard to suppress rapid repeated clicks
3. SUCCESS TOAST — confirm completion without requiring navigation
- Recheck: "Property re-checked — verified today"
- Enrichment request: "Enrichment requested — the analyst will review this property"
- Toast auto-dismisses after 3 seconds
4. ERROR HANDLING — rollback optimistic update + show error toast
- Recheck: freshness badge reverts to previous state (e.g., STALE if it was STALE before)
- Enrichment request: pending badge removed, error toast "Request failed — please try again"
- Button returns to idle state — never gets stuck in loading
5. NO PAGE NAVIGATION — both actions are inline, the page does NOT reload
IMPLEMENTATION:
- Extract shared async state hooks: useAsyncAction() or shared state patterns in a new utils/asyncActions.ts
- Both FE-231 and FE-229 import the same pattern
- Use a lightweight toast library (existing toast system in the project)
- Document the pattern in frontend/src/components/AffordabilityNode.tsx as reference
DEPENDENCIES:
- FE-229 (enrichment modal) and FE-231 (recheck button) must be implemented first
- This task adds the async UX quality gate on top of the basic implementation
- Can be done in parallel with FE-229/FE-231 but must be tested with both
Claimed 2026-04-09

tasks.title: DE: Create PATCH /api/properties/:id — partial field update endpoint for manual property data editing
notes: PURPOSE: Provide a general-purpose server endpoint that allows the frontend to update any editable property field. This is the backend dependency for the manual property data editor (FE-234).
ENDPOINT: PATCH /api/properties/:id
Content-Type: application/json
Body: { <field_name>: <value>, ... } — any subset of editable fields
Response: 200 { updated property object } | 400 { error } | 404
EDITABLE FIELDS (user-editable):
- list_price: number
- realistic_price: number
- sqft: number
- epc: string (A-G)
- tenure: string
- service_charge: number
- ground_rent: number
- lease_years_remaining: number
- floor_level: string
- council_tax_band: string
- market_status: string (active|under_offer|sold_stc|sold_completed|withdrawn|unknown)
- analyst_notes: string (user notes, distinct from analyst notes)
READ-ONLY FIELDS (never updatable by user):
- id, alpha_score, appreciation_potential, price_history, metadata
- pipeline_status (use /api/properties/:id/status)
- property_rank (use /api/properties/:id/rank)
- market_status is user-editable but analyst may override — note in response
GUARDRAILS:
1. Validate field names — reject any field not in the editable list above
2. Validate value types — e.g., sqft must be a positive number, epc must be A-G
3. Set metadata.last_seen = now() and metadata.discovery_count += 1 on any successful update
4. Recalculate alpha_score if any of [sqft, tenure, nearest_tube_distance, list_price] is updated
5. Recalculate price_per_sqm if list_price or sqft is updated
6. On recalculation: UPDATE properties SET alpha_score = <new>, price_per_sqm = <new> WHERE id = ?
7. Log every user edit to a new user_edits table (see below)
USER_EDITS TABLE:
CREATE TABLE property_user_edits (
id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
property_id TEXT,
field TEXT,
old_value TEXT,
new_value TEXT,
edited_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime("now")),
source TEXT DEFAULT "manual_editor"
CREATE INDEX idx_user_edits_property ON property_user_edits(property_id);
WHY A NEW TABLE:
- Enables full audit trail of user corrections
- Analyst can review user edits alongside analyst edits
- Useful for data quality analysis (which fields are most frequently corrected?)
SERVER FILE: server/index.js
Add new route after the existing GET /api/properties/:id handler (around line 517)

tasks.title: UX: Review and redesign Seasonal Market Cycle phase explanations — replace/augment box grid with a compact lookup table
notes: CONTEXT: The Seasonal Market Cycle chart (SeasonalMarketCycle.tsx) is a strong visual — circular ring chart with supply/demand rings, a needle for current month, and phase-coded colors. However, the phase explanation cards (rates page, lines 114-162 in RatesPage.tsx) are verbose and redundant with the tooltips.
PROBLEMS WITH THE CURRENT BOX GRID:
1. Duplication — each phase card shows the same desc that tooltip shows on hover over the chart. User sees it twice.
2. Verbosity — 4 sentences per phase (e.g., "Post-holiday, minimal activity. Buyer leverage highest.") is too much text for a dense dashboard.
3. Low information density — 2-col grid of 5 cards with lots of padding, but only 3 data points per card: name, desc, signal badge.
4. No data — the cards don't show supply/demand index values per phase. The chart has them; the cards don't.
5. The chart tooltip is the better UX — the boxes fight it for attention without adding value.
UX RESEARCH BRIEF — OPTIONS TO EVALUATE:
OPTION A — Compact lookup table (RECOMMENDED)
- Replace the 5-card grid with a single table: columns = [Phase | Months | Supply | Demand | Signal | One-line]
- 5 rows, one per phase. Current phase highlighted row.
- More scannable, more data-dense, eliminates duplication.
- Tooltip stays — provides the detailed desc on demand.
- Example layout:
Phase        | Months   | S  | D  | Signal         | Verdict
Winter Trough | Jan–Feb | 2.5| 2.5| 🟢 Buyer     | Minimal activity, max leverage
Spring Surge | Mar–May | 8.0 | 8.0 | 🔴 Seller    | Peak competition
OPTION B — Phase strip (horizontal single row)
- 5 compact pills in a single horizontal strip, above the chart.
- Each pill: phase color dot + name + months + signal icon.
- Tooltip provides detail on hover.
- Minimal vertical footprint — fits in a tight layout.
- Better when chart is the hero and space is constrained.
OPTION C — Tooltip-only (no cards at all)
- Remove the phase cards entirely. Chart tooltip is sufficient.
- Maximum chart real estate. Cleanest layout.
- Risk: first-time users may not discover the tooltip.
RECOMMENDED APPROACH: Option A (compact table) for /rates page. Option B (phase strip) as an alternative for /market page compact view.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. Phase explanation no longer duplicates tooltip content
2. Each phase shows its month range and supply/demand index averages
3. Current phase is visually distinct (highlighted row or active pill)
4. Signal badge (Buyer Window / Seller Leverage / Neutral) is present
5. Tooltip on chart still works — provides detailed description for users who hover
6. Layout is more compact and data-dense than current 5-card grid
7. Responsive — works on mobile (sm) without horizontal overflow
SCOPE OF REVIEW:
- Evaluate Options A, B, C against the Bloomberg/Linear design language
- Mock up all three approaches in Figma or paper wireframe (or describe in text)
- Recommend ONE approach with rationale
- Specify exact layout, font sizes, and color treatment for the recommendation
DEPENDENCIES: None — chart (VISX-021) and phase sidebar (UX-050) are done
HANDOFF: Write recommendation in DECISIONS.md as a new ADR, then file FE task for implementation
[UX-051] Starting analysis — reviewing current phase cards vs chart tooltip, evaluating A/B/C options.
[UX-051] COMPLETED. ADR-017 written to DECISIONS.md. FE-235 filed for implementation. Option A recommended (compact lookup table). Option B filed as UX-052 follow-on for market page.

tasks.title: FE: edit form all property fields [FE-235]
notes: PURPOSE: The edit form (PropertyEdit.tsx) must include every field in the Property type — not just the ones the analyst happens to have thought of. If a field exists in the database, it must be editable.
REFERENCE: frontend/src/types/property.ts — Property interface is the source of truth for all fields.
REFERENCE: server/index.js — properties table columns (lines 28-66) define the full dataset.
COMPLETE FIELD LIST FOR THE EDIT FORM:
SECTION 1 — FINANCIAL (always editable)
list_price: number input, formatted as £currency
realistic_price: number input, formatted as £currency
service_charge: number input, formatted as £/year
ground_rent: number input, formatted as £/year
council_tax_band: text input (e.g., "D", "E")
SECTION 2 — PROPERTY CHARACTERISTICS
sqft: number input, formatted as integer with sqft suffix
epc: single-select dropdown (A / B / C / D / E / F / G)
tenure: text input (Freehold / Share of Freehold / Leasehold / etc.)
lease_years_remaining: number input (integer, years)
floor_level: text input (e.g., "Ground", "1st", "2nd", "Penthouse", "3rd Floor")
dom: number input (integer — days on market)
SECTION 3 — LOCATION & PROXIMITY (often wrong in scraped data)
nearest_tube_distance: number input (meters — editable, not calculated)
park_proximity: number input (meters — editable, not calculated)
commute_paternoster: number input (minutes)
commute_canada_square: number input (minutes)
waitrose_distance: number input (meters)
whole_foods_distance: number input (meters)
wellness_hub_distance: number input (meters)
SECTION 4 — MARKET DATA
market_status: dropdown (active | under_offer | sold_stc | sold_completed | withdrawn | unknown)
price_reduction_amount: number input (£, optional)
price_reduction_percent: number input (%, optional)
days_since_reduction: number input (integer, optional)
price_reduction_date: date input (optional)
SECTION 5 — RATING & ANALYSIS
epc_improvement_potential: single-select dropdown (A / B / C / D / E / F / G)
est_capex_requirement: number input (£, optional)
neg_strategy: text input (freeform — user negotiation notes)
SECTION 6 — MEDIA
image_url: text input (URL)
floorplan_url: text input (URL)
streetview_url: text input (URL)
links: text area input — one URL per line or comma-separated
SECTION 7 — NOTES (user-editable)
analyst_notes: textarea (user personal notes — label clearly as "Your Notes")
READ-ONLY (show greyed, not editable):
id, address, area, alpha_score, appreciation_potential, price_history, metadata,
pipeline_status (use /api/properties/:id/status), property_rank (use /api/properties/:id/rank),
vetted, is_value_buy, price_per_sqm
Show with tooltip: "This field is calculated — use Re-check or Request Enrichment to update"
INPUT TYPES & VALIDATION:
- Number fields: <input type="number"> with appropriate min/step/formatting
- Currency: formatted display (£XXX,XXX) but submits raw number to PATCH
- Date fields: <input type="date"> — ISO string in PATCH body
- URL fields: <input type="url"> with URL validation
- EPC: styled select with color-coded options (A=green ... G=red)
- market_status: styled select with status color badges per ADR-017
LAYOUT GUIDANCE:
- Do not put all fields in one long scroll — use collapsible accordion sections (already in FE-234)
- Section 1 (Financial) and Section 6 (Media) are most likely to need editing — show expanded by default
- Numeric fields: right-aligned inputs, formatted with £ or m suffix in the input field
- URL fields: show a small preview link icon next to the input
COMMON PITFALLS TO AVOID:
- Do not accidentally skip fields because they seem unlikely to be wrong
- Distance fields (tube, park, Waitrose) are frequently wrong in scraped data — must be editable
- epc_improvement_potential and est_capex_requirement are often missing — must be accessible
- The links field is a JSON array on the backend but should be a textarea for the user
- analyst_notes must be clearly labelled "Your Notes" to distinguish from analyst annotations
DEPENDENCIES: FE-233 (server endpoint), FE-234 (page structure)

tasks.title: BUG: image_url and floorplan_url blocked in PATCH /api/properties/:id — protected list too restrictive
notes: SYMPTOM: User tries to update image_url or floorplan_url via the edit form — server returns 400: { "error": "Protected fields cannot be updated via this endpoint", "attempted_protected_fields": ["image_url", "floorplan_url"] }

ROOT CAUSE: server/index.js line 548-549 — image_url and floorplan_url are in the protected_ blocklist alongside genuinely system-critical fields (archived, pipeline_status, property_rank). They were blocked as a precautionary measure but there is no technical or data-integrity reason to prevent users from editing their own image URLs.
FIX: Move image_url and floorplan_url from the protected_ blocklist to the allowed[] whitelist in server/index.js.
WHY THEY SHOULD BE ALLOWED:
- User-correctable field — user may find better images from agent website
- Same trust level as any other scraped URL the user could correct (e.g., links)
- ADR-015 (Manual Media Management) already approved user image editing as a feature
- No risk to pipeline decisions or analyst flags
AFTER FIX (server/index.js):
- Remove image_url and floorplan_url from the protected_ array (line 548-549)
- Add image_url and floorplan_url to the allowed[] array (line 535-545)
- No other changes needed — existing validation (allowed field names) already handles type checking
NOTE ON gallery AND links:
- gallery stays in protected_ — it is a JSON array and requires special handling (add/remove individual items)
- links stays in protected_ — same reason, comma-separated textarea but stored as JSON array
- Future separate endpoint: PATCH /api/properties/:id/links and PATCH /api/properties/:id/gallery (low priority)
TESTING:
- PATCH /api/properties/:id with { image_url: "https://..." } must return 200
- PATCH /api/properties/:id with { floorplan_url: "https://..." } must return 200
- PATCH with { gallery: [...] } must still return 400 (protected)
- PATCH with { links: [...] } must still return 400 (protected)
DEPENDENCIES: None — this unblocks FE-235 and FE-234 from working correctly

tasks.title: BUG: PriceEvolutionChart — remove generateMockHistory, show no-data state when price_history is absent
notes: COMPONENT: frontend/src/components/PropertyPriceEvolution.tsx
POLICY: No synthetic data. Per REQUIREMENTS.md Rule 13 — The use of synthetic, mock, or placeholder data is strictly forbidden in production datasets. This applies to the UI as well.

a. "No price history available" message
b. A "Request Enrichment" button linking to the enrichment request flow (FE-229)
c. Red/amber freshness indicator: data is missing, analyst should source it
d. Do NOT show the chart section at all — no placeholder, no partial chart
CODE REMOVAL:
- Delete the entire generateMockHistory() function (lines 22-56)
- Delete the fallback in useMemo: replace lines 403-407 with: if (!raw || raw.length < 2) return []; return raw;
- Update the conditional render to handle empty history array: if (history.length < 2) return the no-data component
COMPLIANCE: Fixing this enforces REQUIREMENTS.md Requirement 13 — no synthetic data anywhere in the system. The Data Analyst owns the data gap.

tasks.title: DAT: price_history enrichment — populate for active properties [DAT-190]
notes: PURPOSE: The Price Evolution chart on /property/:id pages must show real data. Any property with missing price_history is a data gap that the analyst must close.
WORKFLOW:
1. Query all properties WHERE price_history is null or empty: SELECT id, address, list_price FROM properties WHERE archived = 0 AND (price_history IS NULL OR price_history = "[]");
2. For each property:
a. Check Rightmove/Zoopla for price reduction history on the listing
b. Check the price_history table directly for any entries: SELECT * FROM price_history WHERE property_id = ? ORDER BY date ASC;
c. If price reductions are documented on the portal, add entries to price_history table
d. If no reduction history available, at minimum insert one entry: { property_id, date: today, price: list_price, status: listed or under_offer }
3. For properties with a price_reduction_amount set: ensure price_history has an entry at the reduction date
PRICE_HISTORY TABLE:
Columns: property_id, date, price, price_per_sqm, status, reduction_pct, days_on_market, london_hpi
At minimum: insert a single entry for each active property so the chart has at least 1 data point
Preferred: 2+ entries so the chart shows a meaningful trajectory (original price → current price)
MARKET STATUS FLAG:
If a property has no price history and cannot be sourced, flag it with a market_status note — do not leave active properties with empty price history.

DEPENDENCY: BUG-003 (remove generateMockHistory from FE) must be done first — once the frontend stops generating fake data, the data gaps become visible and the analyst can see exactly what needs fixing.
REFERENCE: REQUIREMENTS.md Rule 13 — No synthetic, mock, or placeholder data. Price history must be real market data.

tasks.title: DAT: Enrich all active properties with real bedroom and bathroom counts from portal listings
notes: PURPOSE: Source real bedroom and bathroom counts for all active properties. Per REQUIREMENTS.md Rule 13 — no synthetic data. Values must come from live portal listings.
WORKFLOW:
1. Query all properties where bedrooms IS NULL or bathrooms IS NULL:
SELECT id, address, source, links FROM properties WHERE archived = 0 AND (bedrooms IS NULL OR bathrooms IS NULL);
2. For each property with missing data:
a. Open the portal URL from the links field (Rightmove/Zoopla/agent)
b. Extract bedrooms and bathrooms from the listing spec
c. Update: UPDATE properties SET bedrooms = ?, bathrooms = ? WHERE id = ?;
SOURCES TO CHECK:
- Rightmove listing page: "X bed Y bath" in the key details banner
- Zoopla listing page: same position, may be in a "Bathrooms" field
- Agent direct listing (e.g., Savills, Knight Frank): check the property spec table
- Floorplan images: bathroom count can sometimes be inferred from floor plan
- EPC register: not reliable for bedroom/bathroom counts
FIELD SPECIFICATION:
- bedrooms: decimal (1.5 = "1.5 bed" e.g. a double room used as bedroom 2; 2.0 = standard 2-bed; 3.0 = 3-bed)
- bathrooms: decimal (1.0 = 1 bathroom; 1.5 = bathroom + en-suite; 2.0 = 2 bathrooms)
- Do NOT guess — if listing does not disclose, leave as NULL
PRIORITISATION:
- Priority 1: Shortlisted properties (pipeline_status = shortlisted)
- Priority 2: All other active properties
- If a portal URL is broken/not available: PATCH the enrichment request with status=failed and analyst_notes describing the issue
COMPLIANCE: Per REQUIREMENTS.md Rule 13 — no synthetic data. Do not fabricate counts. If unverifiable, leave NULL and flag the property for manual review.
DEPENDENCY: DE-219 (columns added) — enrich only after schema is ready
