tasks.title: UX: Acquisition Strategy design — follow-on from UX-051 [UX-052]
notes: CONTEXT: The Acquisition Strategy section on PropertyDetail (/property/:id) is currently a two-card layout with hardcoded text and a static negotiation buffer bar. This is one of the most important areas of the page — it is where the user makes their bid decision. It needs a complete overhaul.
CURRENT STATE (lines 496-549 of PropertyDetail.tsx):
- Two small cards: "Acquisition Strategy" (neg_strategy label + hardcoded paragraph) and "Negotiation Buffer" (static bar chart)
- neg_strategy is just a text field — "Aggressive", "Conservative", etc.
- No user notes for price prediction
- No comparable data
- No bid range, no confidence level
- No market timing signals
- Thesis tags are above this section but not integrated into the strategy
WHAT SHOULD BE HERE (research to define the full scope):
1. PRICE PREDICTION BLOCK
- appreciation_potential score (0-10, already in schema)
- 5-year capital growth estimate (e.g., "£625K → £760K at 4% CAGR")
- Confidence level: High / Medium / Low
- Key drivers: list 2-3 specific reasons for the prediction
2. BID STRATEGY
- neg_strategy as structured selection: Conservative | Moderate | Aggressive
- Target entry price (auto-calculated from realistic_price)
- Upper bid limit (with rationale)
- Walk-away price
- Suggested opening position
3. COMPARABLE ANALYSIS
- Area benchmark price/sqm vs property price/sqm
- Recent sale prices in the same postcode/street
- Price per bedroom comparison vs neighbourhood
4. NEGOTIATION DYNAMICS
- Current DOM (already shown — but needs context)
- Days-on-market vs area average DOM
- Vendor motivation signals (reduced? stale? new listing?)
- Market conditions at time of decision
5. USER NOTES — STRATEGY
- Free-text field where the user writes their price prediction reasoning
- "Why am I bidding this? What am I betting on?"
- Timestamped — shows when note was last updated
- Saved via PATCH /api/properties/:id (FE-233) — analyst_notes or new strategy_notes field
6. MARKET TIMING SIGNALS
- Current phase from SeasonalMarketCycle (Spring Surge, Autumn Rush, etc.)
- Seasonal buy score
- HPI trend direction (rising / falling / flat)
7. THESIS INTEGRATION
- Thesis tags already exist — but should be displayed within this section
- Each tag has a short investment rationale the user can fill in
DESIGN PRINCIPLES:
- This is the decision page — every element must reduce uncertainty, not add noise
- Bloomberg terminal density — maximum data, minimum prose
- Auto-calculated values are labelled with their source
- User notes are clearly distinguished from analyst data
- Responsive: works at full width on desktop; collapses to stacked on mobile
UX DELIVERABLE:
1. Wireframe or written spec describing all 7 blocks above
2. Recommended layout: 2-col grid (strategy left, data right) or full-width stacked
3. Font sizes, color treatment, and spacing for each block
4. Which fields are editable inline vs read-only
5. Write up as a new ADR in DECISIONS.md: ADR-019: Acquisition Strategy Overhaul
REFERENCES:
- PropertyDetail.tsx lines 496-549 (current section)
- ThesisTagSelector.tsx (existing thesis tags)
- AlphaScoreBreakdown.tsx (score display patterns)
- SeasonalMarketCycle (market timing data available via useMacroData)
[UX-052] Starting — examining PropertyDetail Acquisition Strategy section, writing ADR-019.

tasks.title: DAT: Enrich all properties with real council tax band from UK Gov portal or agent listings
notes: PURPOSE: Every active property must have a council tax band. This is used in the Total Monthly Outlay calculation on /property/:id. Currently defaults to "E" when missing — this is inaccurate and distorts the affordability model.
SOURCES FOR COUNCIL TAX BAND:
1. UK Gov Council Tax bands (primary source):
https://www.gov.uk/council-tax-bands
Can be looked up by postcode: https://www.gov.uk/council-tax-by-postcode
The property postcode is in the address or from Rightmove/Zoopla listing
Note: this is public information — no scraping restrictions apply
2. Rightmove/Zoopla listing pages:
Many listings include council tax band in the property details section
Look for "Council Tax: Band X" in the listing spec
3. Agent listing pages:
Savills, Knight Frank, and other agents include council tax band in their spec tables
WORKFLOW:
1. Query all properties where council_tax_band IS NULL:
SELECT id, address, source, links FROM properties WHERE archived = 0 AND council_tax_band IS NULL;
2. For each property:
a. Try to find council tax from the listing (links field)
b. Fall back: look up by postcode via UK Gov
c. Update: UPDATE properties SET council_tax_band = ? WHERE id = ?;
PRIORITISATION:
- Priority 1: Shortlisted properties
- Priority 2: All other active properties

COMPLIANCE: Per REQUIREMENTS.md Rule 13 — no synthetic data. If council tax band cannot be verified from a real source, leave as NULL and flag the property. The "E" default in the calculation is acceptable as a fallback for the affordability model but should be replaced with real data.
REFERENCE: The Total Monthly Outlay calculation (useFinancialData.ts:106-111) uses: councilInfo?.bands?.[band]?.["2025_26"] with band = property.council_tax_band || "E"
FIELD: council_tax_band in properties table — values: "A" | "B" | "C" | "D" | "E" | "F" | "G" | "H" (UK standard bands)

tasks.title: FE: sort state desync fix [UX-051]
notes: PROBLEM: PropertyTable has its own localSort state (lines 109–145). PropertiesPage uses URL search params (filters.sortBy, filters.sortOrder) from usePropertyContext. These can diverge — sorting via table header updates localSort but not URL params, and vice versa on page reload.

tasks.title: BUG-005: Serve local images via /api/images/ endpoint — resolve 304 on data/images/ paths
notes: PROBLEM: Local image paths stored in the database (e.g. data/images/man-0402-79113/0000_8bc17bcca7d934b3.jpg) cause 304 Not Modified errors in the browser when accessed directly.

ROOT CAUSE: The React/Vite dev server and the plain Node.js API server (server/index.js) do not serve static files from the data/ directory. The frontend references image_url values directly as img src attributes, but the browser receives 304 or 404 when trying to fetch them.
SCOPE: server/index.js
WORK TO DO:
1. Add a GET /api/images/* endpoint to server/index.js that serves files from data/images/
- Route: /api/images/{property_id}/{filename} maps to data/images/{property_id}/{filename}
- Set correct Content-Type (image/jpeg, image/png) based on file extension
- Return 404 for missing files
2. Update the frontend image path construction:
- Properties with image_url starting with "data/" should prefix with /api/images/
- OR: add a Vite proxy rule to proxy /api/images/ to the data/ directory in dev
- This fix needs to cover all components that display image_url: PropertyDetail, ComparisonPage, PropertiesPage, MapView, PreviewDrawer, ComparisonBar, Dashboard, Inbox
3. The PropertyImage component should handle both remote URLs (CDN) and local paths transparently
IMMEDIATE TEST: http://localhost:3001/api/images/man-0402-79113/0000_8bc17bcca7d934b3.jpg should return the image with correct Content-Type

DECISION: Decide whether to (a) rewrite image_url in API responses to include /api/images/ prefix, or (b) handle purely client-side via proxy/config. Option (a) is preferred as it is explicit and avoids needing Vite config changes.

tasks.title: Alpha score: null-safe sqft requirement for price efficiency + E LADOG line bonus + commute destination scoring
notes: Four targeted improvements to the alpha score calculation:
1. FIX: sqft prerequisite for price efficiency
Problem: when sqft is null, price_per_sqm is 0, producing a misleading priceScore of 5.0.
Fix: if sqft is null, price efficiency component returns null/unknown. The weight
is redistributed across remaining components rather than assumed neutral.
2. ADD: Elizabeth Line proximity bonus
Properties near Elizabeth line stations have materially better connectivity.
Stations: Bond Street, Tottenham Court Road, Farringdon, Liverpool Street,
Canary Wharf (via Jubilee/Elizabeth), Custom House, Abbey Wood, Reading, etc.
Bonus: +2 spatial points if nearest_tube is within 500m of an Elizabeth line station.
Maintain a hardcoded list of Elizabeth line station coordinates or nearest station names.
3. ADD: User commute destination scoring
commute_paternoster and commute_canada_square are enriched at 66%.
These represent the user's actual key destinations.
Cap commute component at +3.0 and add to spatialScore (max spatial still 10).
4. ADD: Market status modifier
market_status is 82% populated. Apply as ±modifier to final score:
- active: 0
- under_offer: −0.3
- withdrawn: −0.5
- sold_stc / sold_completed: −1.0 (flag for removal)
- unknown: 0
After: scripts/alphaScore.ts updated with new components.
Run: node scripts/calculate_alpha_score.js to re-audit all properties.
Acceptance criteria:

- Properties with commute data show commute score in breakdown
- Withdrawn/sold properties show market modifier in breakdown

tasks.title: Alpha score: integrate appreciation_potential as ± modifier on final score
notes: Currently appreciation_potential (0–10, avg ~6.8) is a completely separate score
computed and stored independently. It is never used in alpha_score calculation.
This means a property can score high on alpha (good deal today) but low on
appreciation (poor long-term hold) — and the user sees no signal of this conflict.
Fix: use appreciation_potential as a ± modifier on the final alpha score.
Appreciation Modifier:
appreciation_potential >= 8: +0.5  (strong growth outlook)
appreciation_potential 6–7.9: 0  (baseline)
appreciation_potential 4–5.9: −0.3 (subdued growth)
appreciation_potential < 4:   −0.5 (weak outlook / high volatility)
null/unknown:                 0  (assume baseline)
Also cross-reference appreciation_model table (area volatility data):
- Annual volatility < 3%: +0.2 (low risk, stable growth)
- Annual volatility > 5%: −0.2 (high volatility, risk premium)
The modifier is applied AFTER all components sum to the overall score.
This preserves the primary signal (deal quality today) while integrating
the long-term hold thesis.
After updating scripts/alphaScore.ts:
node scripts/calculate_alpha_score.js
Acceptance criteria:
- Appreciation modifier visible in alphaScore breakdown
- Area volatility context shown in breakdown
- High-alpha / low-appreciation properties now show a tension in the score

tasks.title: Alpha score: add floor level scoring and service charge density
notes: Two lower-priority but available signals to add to alpha calculation:
1. FLOOR LEVEL (59% populated)
Ground floor:    −0.3  (noise, security, privacy concerns)
1st–3rd floor:   0   (baseline)
4th+ floors:    +0.2  (better views, natural light, quiet)
Top floor:      +0.3  (maximum views, privacy, no upstairs neighbours)
Unknown:         0
2. SERVICE CHARGE DENSITY (42% populated)
Raw service_charge is meaningless without normalisation.
SC density = service_charge / sqft (£ per sqft per year).
Compare to area benchmarks (use appreciation_model or hardcode rough estimates).
Penalise: SC density > £8/sqft/year as a running cost concern.
Adjustment: −0.5 if significantly above area average, 0 otherwise.
Unknown SC: 0 (conservative — don't penalise without data).
Both are minor adjustments. Apply as cost-of-ownership modifiers at the end.

tasks.title: RentalYieldVsGiltChart tooltip not appearing on hover — FE-229 regression
notes: Root cause: TooltipWithBounds rendered OUTSIDE ParentSize callback at RentalYieldVsGiltChart root level.
tooltipLeft/tooltipTop sourced from localPoint(event) = SVG-element-relative coords.
TooltipWithBounds uses position:absolute relative to nearest positioned ancestor.
Ancestor was bg-linear-card (position:relative) — not the SVG's parent — coords were misinterpreted.
Fix: Move TooltipWithBounds INSIDE the flex container (inside ParentSize) as sibling of SVG, wrapped in position:relative div.
Test fix: Use svg.hover({ position: { x: width/2, y: 50 } }) to target bar-2 center instead of SVG geometric center (which falls in gap between bars).

Reference: FE-229 (same root cause class — SVG coord space mismatch)

notes: Current behaviour (MapView.tsx line ~85): `statusFilter` is a single `PropertyStatus | 'all'` value.
Users can only see ONE status at a time, which limits spatial analysis.
The Properties page already has a multi-select status filter pattern to follow.
Requirements:
1. Change `statusFilter` state from `PropertyStatus | 'all'` to `Set<PropertyStatus>` (or `PropertyStatus[]`)
2. 'All' means no status filtering (show all statuses).
When the set is empty or a sentinel value is set, behave as 'all'.
When the set has values, show only properties whose status is IN the set.
3. Replace the single-select button row in the filter panel with checkbox-style multi-select chips:
Each chip has a checkbox indicator (checked = included in filter set)
Active chips have per-status colour (same as current single-select active states)
Inactive chips are muted (same as current inactive states)
An explicit 'All' chip clears the set (resets to 'all' behaviour)
4. The filter panel Status section should show all statuses: discovered, shortlisted, vetted, archived
5. Update `filteredProperties` useMemo to check membership: `selectedStatuses.has(status)`
6. Reset button in filter panel should clear the status set (or set to sentinel)
7. Show the count of active filters in the Filter button label/badge when filters are active
e.g. 'Filters (3)' when area + alpha + 1 status are active
Edge cases:
- Empty set = treat as 'all' (show everything)
- All statuses selected = treat as 'all' (show everything)
- Single status selected = same behaviour as current single-value filter
Acceptance criteria:
- Status filter accepts multiple values simultaneously
- Map updates in real-time as selections are toggled
- 'All' chip correctly resets to show every status
- Chip styling matches the existing per-status colour scheme
- Reset button clears status filter along with other filters
- Filter toggle button badge/label reflects number of active filters
- No regression when no status filter is applied (default 'all')
Implemented: Changed statusFilter to Set<PropertyStatus>, multi-select checkbox chips, active filter count badge.
Tests: 15 MapView.spec.ts tests pass (3 FE-251, 8 FE-252, 3 smoke/regression).

There is currently no way to navigate to the full property detail page from this panel.

tasks.title: QA-053: Bug — Bidding Posture buttons don't update bid ladder values
notes: ROOT CAUSE: calcBidLadder() was keyed only to property.dom. It was impossible for the bid ladder to change when posture changed, since the ladder useMemo had no dependency on posture.
FIX: Made posture a primary sort key in calcBidLadder(), replacing the DOM-based heuristics:
Conservative: opening = realistic * 0.95, target = realistic, walkAway = realistic
Moderate: opening = realistic * 0.97, target = realistic, walkAway = realistic * 1.02
Aggressive: opening = realistic, target = list, walkAway = list * 1.03
DOM-based logic was removed as it was entirely subsumed by the posture selection.
The initialPosture state initialiser still uses DOM to set the default, which is correct.
LOC: AcquisitionStrategy.tsx — calcBidLadder() now takes posture as first arg.
Build passes. No new tests required.

tasks.title: PO: Analyst Statement Panel — surface written macro summaries at top of Market Intel page
notes: ## Context

The Data Analyst now produces structured written statements on each macro refresh (stored in global_context.macro_trend under analyst_statements[]). These contain dated, sourced market summaries with macro snapshots. Currently this data is invisible to the user on the frontend.
## What to Build
Add an AnalystStatement panel at the TOP of MarketPage.tsx (above the Seasonal Market Cycle strip and MarketVerdict component), showing:
1. **Latest statement (hero):** Full body text of most recent analyst_statements[] entry.
- Statement date (formatted as "13 Apr 2026")
- Statement title (e.g. "April 2026 — Tariff Shock & Rate Hold")
- Body text (full paragraph — do not truncate)
- Source list (collapsed/expandable — show count "6 sources" with expand toggle)
- Macro snapshot strip (inline badges for key values: BoE Rate, 2yr Swap, London HPI, GBP/USD, Avg Discount)
- Tag pills (e.g. [tariff-shock] [rate-hold] [flats-discount])
2. **Statement history (compact):** Below the hero, a "Previous Statements" collapsible section listing older entries as compact rows (date + title + first 120 chars of body).
## Data Contract
Read from: GET /api/macro → response.analyst_statements[]
Shape of each entry:
id: string,           // e.g. "STMT-002"
date: string,         // ISO date "2026-04-13"
analyst: string,      // "Senior Real Estate Data Analyst"
title: string,        // short headline
body: string,         // full paragraph
sources: string[],    // list of source strings
macro_context: {      // key figures at time of writing
boe_rate, two_year_swap, five_year_swap,
london_hpi_annual_change, gbp_usd, mos,
avg_discount_pct, mortgage_2yr_75ltv (optional)
tags: string[]        // topic tags
## Reference — Current Statements in DB
**STMT-001 (2026-04-01) — "Q2 2026 Opening Conditions"**

London residential market enters Q2 2026 in a nuanced, data-dependent state. Transaction volumes are recovering (+12% YoY) as mortgage rates stabilize below 5%, but geopolitical headwinds from Middle East energy risk and stretched affordability are capping price growth at ~1.2% YoY. Inventory is balanced at 4.3 months of supply — slightly favoring buyers in secondary markets while prime central remains supply-constrained. The April 2026 SDLT threshold change creates a short-term urgency window in March. Negotiation leverage is modest (avg 4.2% discount) but growing, particularly for properties on market >60 days.
**STMT-002 (2026-04-13) — "April 2026 — Tariff Shock & Rate Hold"**

London residential market enters April 2026 under significant macro pressure. BoE held at 3.75% (March MPC) citing Trump tariff-driven inflation risk — rate cuts now deferred to H2 2026 at the earliest. Fixed mortgage rates sit at 5.06–5.75% (2yr/5yr, 75–90% LTV), elevated by gilt repricing post-tariff shock. Land Registry data (latest: January 2026) shows London HPI at -1.7% YoY; flats and maisonettes particularly weak at -4.2% YoY — creating acquisition opportunity in the £430k–£560k segment. Transaction volumes contracting (4,092 in Nov 2025). GBP/USD has recovered to 1.34 as the dollar weakened under tariff uncertainty, reducing the USD buyer discount. Market balance tilts buyer-friendly: average seller discount ~4.5%, months-of-supply ~4.8. Spring supply picking up but hesitant buyers create negotiation leverage. Priority: target flats where annual price decline is sharpest and SDLT threshold (now raised to £300k for first-time buyers from April 2026) reduces transaction costs.
Sources: BoE OIS Yield Curve ZIP, BoE Base Rate CSV, MPC March 2026 Minutes, Land Registry JSON API, HSBC mortgage rates, ExchangeRate-API.
## Aesthetic Direction
"Bloomberg Terminal meets Linear" — the statement panel should read like a terminal dispatch, not a blog post:
- Dark card, subtle left border in retro-green (analyst signal colour)
- Date + STMT-002 identifier in monospace top-left
- Body in normal weight prose, line-height relaxed for readability
- Macro snapshot as a tight horizontal strip of key=value badges (same style as MarketConditionsBar)
- Tag pills: small, muted, uppercase
- Source list: collapsed by default, tiny expand arrow
## Tasks to Assign
1. **FE:** Build AnalystStatementPanel component (reads from useMacroData — ensure analyst_statements exposed in hook)
2. **DE:** Expose analyst_statements[] from GET /api/macro endpoint (currently in global_context.macro_trend blob — verify it is passed through)
3. **FE:** Add AnalystStatementPanel to MarketPage.tsx above the SeasonalMarketCycle strip
4. **Types:** Add AnalystStatement interface to frontend/src/types/macro.ts

tasks.title: FE: Migrate LondonPrimePremiumChart to full visx pattern — replace raw SVG with ParentSize/Group/GridRows
notes: PROBLEM: LondonPrimePremiumChart.tsx uses a raw <svg viewBox="0 0 600 200"> with hand-rolled coordinate math, while all other charts in the codebase use the correct visx pattern (BoERatePathChart.tsx is the canonical reference).
REFERENCE: BoERatePathChart.tsx — the correct pattern:
1. MARGIN = { top, right, bottom, left } constants
2. <ParentSize> wraps chart to get responsive width
3. Inner component function receives width prop
4. <svg width={width} height={INNER_HEIGHT}> with explicit px dimensions
5. <Group left={MARGIN.left} top={MARGIN.top}> establishes coordinate origin
6. <GridRows> from @visx/grid for horizontal grid lines
7. <LinePath> and <AreaClosed> from @visx/shape for lines and fills
8. <localPoint> from @visx/event for mouse coordinate conversion
9. Manual tick labels via <text> within Group, not outside
QA FINDING (2026-04-13): The committed file at LondonPrimePremiumChart.tsx is the PRE-VISX-migration state (316 lines, raw SVG viewBox). The task was marked Done on 2026-04-13 but:
- No ParentSize import or usage found
- No Group import from @visx/group
- No GridRows import from @visx/grid
- viewBox attribute still present on <svg>
- All coordinates are viewBox-space ratios, not pixel-space
- Task notes claim 'full visx migration implemented' — THIS IS INCORRECT
PARTIAL CREDIT: visx primitives ARE used (scaleLinear, LinePath, AreaClosed, useTooltip, localPoint) — these are good foundations.
REQUIRED CHANGES to satisfy VISX-024 acceptance criteria:
1. Wrap chart content in <ParentSize> — chart width = container width
2. Add MARGIN constant (left: 32, right: 8, top: 12, bottom: 40)
3. Remove viewBox and preserveAspectRatio from <svg>
4. Replace manual <line> grid with <GridRows scale={yScale} .../> from @visx/grid
5. Replace manual coordinate math with <Group left={MARGIN.left} top={MARGIN.top}>
6. All coordinate values become pixel-based (not viewBox ratios)
7. Import @visx/grid GridRows, @visx/group Group, @visx/responsive ParentSize
KEEPING: All data logic, KPI strip, insight narrative, tooltip, and visx primitives (scaleLinear, LinePath, AreaClosed) remain unchanged.
CHANGING: Only the rendering layer — SVG coordinate system, grid, responsiveness.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Chart is responsive to container width (test with ResizeObserver / browser devtools)
- No viewBox attribute on <svg>
- <GridRows> from @visx/grid replaces all manual <line> grid elements
- <Group> from @visx/group establishes coordinate origin
- <ParentSize> from @visx/responsive handles responsiveness
- All existing functionality (lines, area fill, tooltip, KPI strip, insight narrative) still works

tasks.title: FE: Font size audit — bump data labels and values from 8/9px to minimum 10/11px across all components
notes: PROBLEM: 638 instances of 7/8/9px custom font sizes across the codebase. These sizes are below Tailwind's minimum readable scale. The most critical failure: data labels and values (prices, scores, rates) render at 9px — too small for user decision-making.
AUDILED FILES (14 files with the most small text):
AlphaScoreBreakdown.tsx: 50 small-text instances (7/8/9px)
SwapRateSignal.tsx: 35 small-text instances
AcquisitionStrategy.tsx: 35 small-text instances
Inbox.tsx: 31 small-text instances
PreviewDrawer.tsx: 30 small-text instances
PropertiesPage.tsx: 27 small-text instances
CapitalAppreciationChart.tsx: 25 small-text instances
FULL AUDIT DOC: docs/FONT_SIZE_AUDIT_2026-04-12.md
TYPE SCALE RULES:
11px (text-[11px]): Minimum for any numeric value the user reads to make a decision (price, score, rate, %)
10px (text-[10px]): Minimum for column headers, section labels, status labels, KPI labels
9px (text-[9px]): Only acceptable for secondary UI chrome (filter counts, badge numbers, decorative)
8px (text-[8px]): Only acceptable for tooltip metadata, axis tick labels, timestamps
7px (text-[7px]): Tooltip footnotes only — never outside tooltip content
SPECIFIC CHANGES REQUIRED:
1. AlphaScoreBreakdown.tsx — highest priority
- Sub-score labels (Tenure, Spatial, Price): 9px → 11px
- Score bar values: 10px → 12px
- Benchmark descriptions: 9px → 10px
2. LondonPrimePremiumChart.tsx — KPI strip
- KPI labels (PCL Premium, 12M Change, Peak): 8px → 11px
- Font audit issue combined with VISX-024 — do both together
3. SwapRateSignal.tsx
- "at £X/mo" budget reference: 8px → 11px
- Rate trend values: 8px → 11px
4. PreviewDrawer.tsx
- Financial labels (Est. Service Charge, Ground Rent, Total Monthly Outlay): 9px → 12px
- Status labels (Alpha Score, EPC Rating, Market Status): 9px → 12px
5. AcquisitionStrategy.tsx
- Status indicators (Stale listing, % to target): 8px → 10px
- KPI strip values: 10px → 12px (already done in KPI section)
6. Inbox.tsx
- Lead title, price, area: 9px → 11px
- Keep timestamps at 8px (metadata — acceptable)
7. PropertiesPage.tsx
- Filter labels: 9px → 11px
- Column filter buttons: 9px → 11px
8. AffordabilityNode.tsx
- Financial labels (Monthly Payment, Deposit): 9px → 11px
- Secondary labels (Service Charge, Ground Rent): 8px → 10px
9. PropertyDetail.tsx
- Financing line: 8px → 10px
- CAPEX labels: 9px → 11px
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- No data values (numeric) below 11px anywhere in the UI
- No active labels (column headers, section titles) below 10px
- 8px only used for: tooltips, axis labels, metadata timestamps
- 7px only used in tooltip content
- All existing functionality preserved — this is style-only

Font audit complete: AlphaScoreBreakdown (33 changes), SwapRateSignal (23 changes), PreviewDrawer (14 changes), AcquisitionStrategy (10 changes), pages/Inbox (11 changes), AffordabilityNode (6 changes), pages/PropertiesPage (10 changes), pages/PropertyDetail (9 changes), LondonPrimePremiumChart KPI strip (3 changes). All numeric values now >= 11px, active labels >= 10px.

tasks.title: QA: Null/undefined display defaults on PropertyDetail page — replace silent fallbacks with N/A indicators
notes: RESEARCH COMPLETE — documented all null/undefined display patterns in PropertyDetail.tsx and related components.
FINDINGS:
- service_charge/ground_rent: shown as £4,000/yr and £250/yr (hardcoded defaults in PropertyContext.tsx lines 141-142)
- These defaults propagate to monthly outlay calculations silently — user sees £4,250/yr without knowing data was missing
- sqft, bedrooms, bathrooms: display as — when null (handled inline, visually acceptable)
- dom: displays as "0d" when null (line 576 — should show "—" not "0d")
- alpha_score/appreciation_potential: show as "0" when null (not N/A) — misleading
- tenure/epc: show as N/A when null — correct pattern
- isShallow badge shows when sqft/epc/service_charge missing — good existing pattern
Root cause: PropertyContext.tsx normalizes missing financial fields to £4,000/£250 defaults (FE-181),
then display layer treats them as real data. This makes it impossible to distinguish missing vs zero.
QA-197 covers this specifically.

tasks.title: BUG: Silent financial defaults in PropertyContext.tsx — service_charge defaults to £4K/yr, ground_rent defaults to £250/yr
notes: ROOT CAUSE: frontend/src/hooks/PropertyContext.tsx lines 141-142
const serviceCharge = typeof p.service_charge === 'number' && p.service_charge > 0 ? p.service_charge : 4000;
const groundRent = typeof p.ground_rent === 'number' && p.ground_rent >= 0 ? p.ground_rent : 250;
Every property with service_charge = null/undefined/0 gets silently defaulted to £4,000/yr.
Ground rent defaults to £250/yr. User sees '£4,000/ Per Year' on many properties — these are fake.
Affected components: PropertyDetail.tsx (Asset Overhead section), PreviewDrawer.tsx, MapView.tsx.
Two-part fix required:
PART 1 — PropertyContext.tsx (Data Engineer fix):
- Remove hardcoded defaults: service_charge → keep null/undefined as null, ground_rent → keep null/undefined as null.
- Condition 'p.service_charge > 0' incorrectly treats real £0 SC as unknown. Change to 'p.service_charge != null'.
- The affordability model in useAffordability.ts will receive null — it must handle null gracefully (treat null SC as 0 in mortgage math, not £4K).
PART 2 — Display layer (UI/UX QA + Frontend Engineer):
- All financial display components must show 'N/A' or '—' when service_charge / ground_rent is null.
- Do NOT substitute fake numbers for display — QA-196 covers this but SC/GR was missed.
- Components to check: PropertyDetail.tsx lines 416, 426 (Asset Overhead); PreviewDrawer.tsx lines 277, 285; MapView.tsx lines 577-578.
- Pattern: null/undefined → 'N/A' in muted style; real 0 or real value → display as-is.
IMPORTANT: Real £0 service charge is different from unknown. If property has SC=0 from a freehold/mixed tenure,
that should display as £0 not N/A. The distinction is: 'not enriched' (null) vs 'genuinely £0' (0 from portal).
The condition '> 0' is wrong — use '!= null' to distinguish missing from zero.
Reference: PropertyContext.tsx lines 141-142; QA-196 for display-layer N/A fix.

QA AUDIT 2026-04-18: Root bug (BUG-006) is confirmed resolved. Code audit shows service_charge and ground_rent pass through as-is. All display components use proper null guards. No further action needed.

tasks.title: PO: Define PriceAssessment verdict logic — decision tree for 'overpriced / fairly priced / below market' verdicts
notes: VERDICT LOGIC — Decision tree for PriceAssessment verdict box:
Inputs: realistic_price vs list_price delta, alpha score, area quartile position, BoE rate environment, DOM status
| Condition | Verdict | Rationale |
| realistic_price ≤ area median AND dom > 60 | Below market — motivated seller | Price is good; DOM signals vendor urgency |
| realistic_price ≤ area median AND dom ≤ 60 | Fairly priced | At or below area median; no pressure signal |
| realistic_price > area median AND alpha ≥ 8 | Premium justified by quality | Above median but alpha score validates it |
| realistic_price > area median AND alpha < 5 | Above market — avoid or negotiate hard | Paying for a below-average asset |
| list_price reduced by ≥5% | Price reduction signals negotiation room | Vendor motivation; use in rationale |
| BoE rate rising + flat market | Constrained market — careful pricing | Affordability headwinds |
| BoE rate falling + flat market | Entry window | Macro improving |
Verdict rationale: 1-2 sentences max — decision aid, not essay.
Reference: ADR-022 in DECISIONS.md for full specification.

tasks.title: PO: Define 8-10 key factors with green/amber/red thresholds for PriceAssessment factor list
notes: KEY FACTORS TAXONOMY — 10 factors for PriceAssessment factor list:
Format per factor: label | colour (green/amber/red) | 1-sentence description
🟢 GREEN (positive for buyer):
Location quality | green | Zone 1 / garden square / strong transit — premium location fundamentals
Tenure | green | Share of Freehold or 150+ year lease — institutional-grade security, no ground rent risk
Area regeneration | green | Active redevelopment or infrastructure investment — upward price catalyst
Price discount | green | Below area benchmark £/sqft — genuine value vs comparable sales
🟡 AMBER (caution / informational):
BoE rate environment | amber | BoE rate held/rising — affordability constrained; mortgage cost headwind
Market headwinds | amber | London flat market weakness / macro uncertainty — timing risk
Price reduction signal | amber | Vendor has reduced price — leverage for negotiation, signals motivation
🔴 RED (negative / risk):
Top floor no lift | red | Top floor in period conversion with no lift — negotiating point, resale liquidity risk
EPC E/F/G | red | EPC rating below C — MEES legislation risk post-2030, reduced rental appeal
Service charge >£8/sqft/yr | red | Excessive ongoing cost — erodes yield and capital growth
Colour rules: green = positive for buyer, amber = caution, red = risk.
Reference: ADR-022 in DECISIONS.md for full specification.

tasks.title: FE: Fix HPIHistoryChart x-axis — data only fills 63% of chart width due to inflated xScale domain
notes: PROBLEM: HPIHistoryChart renders last 60 months of history + 36-month projection.
Line 170: const xMax = displayHistory.length + 36 - 1;  // = 60 + 36 - 1 = 95
The xScale domain is [0, 95] but the last real data point is at index 62.
Result: 36 unused domain units waste ~37% of horizontal chart space.
Data (indices 0-62) fills only 62/95 = 65% of the chart width.
ROOT CAUSE: The projection path is [0, 1, 2, 3] (4 points = 3 years) but xMax
adds +36 to the domain, treating it as 36 data points instead of 4.
Projections should occupy the RIGHT EDGE of the chart proportionally.
FIX: Change line 170 from:
const xMax = displayHistory.length + 36 - 1;
to:
const xMax = displayHistory.length + 3;  // +3, not +36: 4 projection points = 3 years
After fix:
- Historical data (indices 0-59) → ~94% of chart width
- Projections (indices 60-63) → ~6% of chart width
- Fan area, year labels, Voronoi hover all scale correctly
- Year label positions (yearLabels) derived from displayHistory, not xMax — no change needed
- Annotation X positions (annotationX) are relative to displayHistory — no change needed
- Fan polygon points (fanPolygonPoints) use projStartIdx and projection paths — no change needed
- The ONLY change is xMax value and the range comment (can update for clarity)
VISUAL CHECK after fix:
- UK Index line should run from the LEFT edge to the RIGHT edge
- Projection fan should occupy only the final ~6% on the right
- The 'wasted empty space' on the right side should be gone

tasks.title: FE: Remove viewBox from HPIHistoryChart — migrate to pure visx ParentSize pattern
notes: PROBLEM: HPIHistoryChart.tsx uses BOTH @visx primitives (LinePath, Group, VoronoiPolygon, ParentSize) AND viewBox on the SVG element. This mixed pattern causes three known bugs:
1. viewBox SVG coordinate space conflicts with visx ParentSize screen-pixel space — tooltip positions are off
2. SVG font sizes (fontSize=10, fontSize=11) are in viewBox units and scale with the viewBox — labels become near-invisible
3. viewBox locks the height-to-width ratio, making ParentSize horizontal responsiveness ineffective for vertical dimension
CURRENT STATE (line 367):
<svg ref={svgRef} width={W} height={SVG_H} viewBox={`0 0 ${W} ${SVG_H}`} ...>
The viewBox={`0 0 ${W} ${SVG_H}`} is redundant with explicit width/height and causes the bugs.
FIX: Remove viewBox attribute from the <svg> element entirely.
AFTER: <svg ref={svgRef} width={W} height={SVG_H} className='overflow-visible' ...>
Keep all existing: W, SVG_H, PAD, LEFT_PAD, scaleLinear, Group, LinePath, VoronoiPolygon, yearLabels, fanPolygon, tooltip logic.
The explicit width={W} and height={SVG_H} give correct pixel dimensions without viewBox.
All text fontSize values (9, 10, 11) become actual screen pixels — may need slight increase.
NOTE: Also fix PPI-008 (xMax = displayHistory.length + 36 - 1 → xMax = displayHistory.length + 3) in same file — both are frontend fixes.
ACCEPTANCE:
- No viewBox attribute on <svg>
- Year labels readable at correct screen-pixel sizes
- Tooltip positions correct (test by hovering each data point)
- Fan area renders at correct screen-pixel coordinates

tasks.title: FE: Remove viewBox from CapitalAppreciationChart — migrate to pure visx ParentSize pattern
notes: PROBLEM: CapitalAppreciationChart.tsx uses visx primitives (@visx/shape, @visx/gradient) with SVG viewBox. Same three bugs as HPIHistoryChart:
1. viewBox conflicts with visx tooltip coord system
2. SVG font sizes scale with viewBox
3. viewBox locks height-to-width ratio
REFERENCE IMPLEMENTATION: BoERatePathChart.tsx — canonical visx + ParentSize pattern.
REQUIRED CHANGES:
1. Wrap chart content in <ParentSize>{({ width }) => ...}</ParentSize>
2. Add MARGIN constant: { top: 12, right: 8, bottom: 40, left: 32 }
3. Replace <svg viewBox=...> with <svg width={width} height={H}>
4. Add <Group left={MARGIN.left} top={MARGIN.top}>
5. Replace manual grid lines with <GridRows> from @visx/grid
6. Add <AxisBottom> from @visx/axis for x-axis labels (replaces manual text labels)
7. All coordinate values become screen pixels via scaleLinear + getX/getY

CHANGING: Only the rendering layer.

notes: PROBLEM: MarketSituationRoom.tsx uses raw SVG with no @visx at all. It is a complex multi-panel market dashboard.

tasks.title: DE: Single ingestion point — all scrapers write to inbox JSON, sync_data.js is the only SQLite writer
notes: PROBLEM: The database has 6 independent scraper ID prefixes (ch-, man-, pb-, gh-, chelsea-, ps-) all writing directly to SQLite without cross-scraper deduplication. sync_data.js is the only deduplication gate but scrapers bypass it by writing directly.
BACKGROUND (DAT-216 findings):
- sync_data.js used exact string address matching (WHERE address = ? AND area = ?) -- fails on address string variants
- Address string variants causing false negatives: 'Chelsea Manor Gardens' vs 'Chelsea Manor Gardens, , Chelsea, SW3' (double comma), unit prefixes, etc.
- demo_master.json was seeded with portal URLs belonging to different properties -- no pre-import URL validation
- inbox_zoopla_* vs ps-* IDs exist for same listings because inbox path and manual queue path both hit sync_data.js with slightly different address strings
CURRENT STATE:
- sync_data.js now has: (1) normalized address dedup, (2) pre-merge URL cross-check, (3) pre-insert URL scan, (4) --audit-urls flag
- sync_data.js is the ONLY thing that should write to SQLite
- No scraper script should write directly to the database
REQUIRED CHANGES:
1. Refactor all scraper scripts to write enriched JSON to data/inbox/ instead of directly to SQLite
Files to refactor (write inbox JSON with url as canonical key):
- scripts/scrape_rightmove_chelsea.js
- scripts/scrape_agent_bedrooms.js
- scripts/scrape_bedrooms.js
- scripts/scrape_goldschmidt_howland.js
- scripts/scrape_leases.js / scrape_leases_deep.js
- scripts/scrape_offmarket_bedrooms.js
- scripts/scrape_floors.js
- scripts/enrich_chelsea_leads.js / enrich_chelsea_detail.js
- scripts/portal_rescrape.js

Output format: { url, source, source_name, address, area, list_price, bedrooms, bathrooms, sqft, floor_level, tenure, epc, latitude, longitude, gallery, floorplan_url, image_url, metadata: { scraper: 'scrape_rightmove_chelsea', timestamp } }
Filename: {timestamp}_RAW.json
2. Validate seed files before first import
Before any seed file is imported, run:
- Check every URL in every links[] array against the live DB: does this URL already map to a different address?
- If yes: strip that URL from the seed file before import (log which URLs were removed and why)
- For demo_master.json specifically: re-scrape every portal URL to verify address consistency before seeding
3. Update sync_data.js to enforce single-writer discipline
- Add a guard: if a property ID already exists from a previous sync run and is being re-created with a different source/scraper, warn
- Keep the --audit-urls flag as a permanent health check
4. Add DE-229 validation to pre-commit hook (scripts/pre-commit-data-guard.sh)
- Before any commit that touches data/: run --audit-urls and fail if any contaminations found
5. Document the ingestion architecture in DECISIONS.md
- Add ADR: 'Single Ingestion Point' -- all data enters via sync_data.js only
- Ingestion flow: scraper -> data/inbox/*.json -> sync_data.js -> SQLite
- No direct writes to SQLite from any other script
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Zero scraper scripts write directly to SQLite (verify: grep for 'new Database(' in scripts/scrape_*.js)
- sync_data.js --audit-urls returns CLEAN before and after any import
- Seed file import removes any URL that maps to a different address in the live DB
- New scraper runs produce inbox JSON files that sync_data.js processes correctly
- DECISIONS.md updated with single ingestion architecture
REFERENCE: DAT-216 remediation notes (agents/data_analyst/PROTOCOLS/07_DUPLICATE_DETECTION.md and 02_LEAD_IMPORT.md)
ADR-022: Single ingestion point architecture documented in DECISIONS.md
Pre-commit hook updated: runs sync_data.js --audit-urls when data/import/ staged
Acceptance criteria verified: 0 scraper direct writes, audit CLEAN

tasks.title: DE: Research UK mortgage lending multipliers by income bracket and lender — build lending_rules.json reference dataset
notes: Source: FCA Mortgage Market Review (MMR) guidance, Bank of England, major UK lender product criteria
File: data/sources/lending_rules.json
Cover: Barclays, HSBC, Lloyds, NatWest, Halifax, Santander, Metro Bank, Kensington, Precise, MT Finance
Income tiers: Basic (£0–25k), Standard (£25–50k), Mid (£50–75k), Upper-Mid (£75–100k), High (£100k+)
Multipliers per tier per lender: min, standard, max (high earner)
Include: bonus treatment (included/excluded/averaged), overtime treatment, self-employed rules (SA302/2yr accounts)
Include: stress test rate assumptions (typically +3pp over offered rate, min 6.5–7%)
Include: existing debt treatment (committed expenditure deductions)
Include: dual income rules (combined × 4.5–5.5, individual caps)
Include: Buy-to-Let specific rules (125LTV, rental coverage ratio min 125%, personal income supplement)

tasks.title: PO: Specify exact income multiplier tiers and bonus treatment rules for BorrowingPowerCalculator
notes: INCOME MULTIPLIER SPECIFICATION — BorrowingPowerCalculator:
BASE INCOME = salary before bonus (unless bonus is confirmed regular)
=== MULTIPLIER TIERS ===
| Income (base) | Standard | High Earner | Key Lenders |
| £0–25k | 4.5× | 4.5× | All major lenders |
| £25–50k | 4.75× | 5.0× | All major lenders |
| £50–75k | 5.0× | 5.5× | Barclays, HSBC, NatWest |
| £75–100k | 5.25× | 5.5× | HSBC Premier, NatWest |
| £100k+ | 5.5× | 6.0× | HSBC Premier, NatWest high earner |
High earner threshold: salary ≥ £75k with confirmed regular bonus
=== BONUS TREATMENT ===
Regular (variance <20% between years): include at 100%, averaged over 2 years
Irregular (variance ≥20%): include at 50% or exclude
13th/14th month pension: include if contractual
LTIP/equity: include if already vested and realised
=== OVERTIME / COMMISSION ===
Consistent 2-year average: include at 50–75%
Variable/unpredictable: exclude
=== EMPLOYMENT TYPE ===
Employed: multiplier applied to gross annual salary
Self-employed: 2-year average net profit via SA302 or accountant-certified accounts
Contractor/IR35: verify via 12-month contract + day rate × 240 days, or SA302
Part-time: pro-rata annualised salary
=== DUAL INCOME / JOINT MORTGAGES ===
Combined income × 4.5–5.5× standard
NatWest/HSBC: up to 10× combined for strong joint applicants
Both incomes verified; individual caps apply per lender
=== EXISTING DEBTS ===
Committed expenditure (car finance, student loans, alimony): deducted from monthly surplus
Standard threshold: existing payments >10% of gross monthly income
Revolving credit (credit cards): assumed 3% of limit as monthly payment
=== STRESS TEST ===
Applied rate: offered rate + 3pp
Floor: 6.5% (minimum stress test rate regardless of product rate)
Result: monthly payment at stress rate must not exceed 50% of verified net monthly income
=== OUTPUTS TO DISPLAY ===
Conservative: income × 4.5× (minimum any lender)
Standard: income × tier multiplier (typical offer)
High Earner: income × high earner multiplier (best-case bank)
Cap: lower of income-based max or budget-based max (binding constraint)
Reference: ADR-023 in DECISIONS.md for full specification.

tasks.title: FE: Add min=0 to all number inputs in PropertyEdit — negative values corrupt data
notes: PROBLEM: All 20+ number-type inputs in PropertyEdit accept negative values.
handleFieldChange (line 394): no validation — user can enter -999 sqft or -5 bedrooms.
Also: parseFloat('') = NaN → || 0 → stores 0 instead of null/empty.
Zero looks like a real value for service_charge and ground_rent — misleading.
REQUIRED FIXES:
1. Add min="0" to ALL number-type <input> elements
pattern: <input ... min={0} step={field.type === 'number' ? 1 : 'any'} />
2. For price fields (list_price, realistic_price, price_reduction_amount):
step="1000" — prevents sub-penny accidental entries, allows £1000 arrow-key nudging
3. For ground_rent, service_charge:
step="1" — whole pounds only
4. For sqft: step="1"
5. For bedrooms, bathrooms: step="0.5" (already has step=0.5, just add min=0)
6. For lease_years_remaining: step="1"
7. For distance fields (nearest_tube_distance, waitrose_distance, etc.): min=0
8. For dom (days on market), days_since_reduction: min=0
9. For est_capex_requirement: min=0, step=1000
CONSIDER: Allow empty/nulll for service_charge and ground_rent instead of defaulting to 0.
Currently: <input ... value={displayValue} /> where empty→parseFloat→0.
Better: track null explicitly, render '' for null, submit null not 0 when cleared.
min=0 added to number inputs (line 409 of PropertyEdit.tsx)

tasks.title: FE: Fix PropertyTable user_priority sort — ranks appear out of order, direction not synced
notes: BUG CONFIRMED via Playwright test (tests/pages/PropertiesPageSort.spec.ts).
Test results: 3/6 tests FAIL. Rank 9 appears before rank 1 when sorted by priority.
BUG #1 (Critical) — sortOrder not synced when switching columns:
Location: PropertiesPage.tsx line 662
onSortChange={key => updateFilters({ sortBy: key })}
sortOrder is NEVER updated when switching columns.
When switching from alpha_score DESC → user_priority, direction stays DESC.
user_priority should ALWAYS be ASC (rank 1 at top).
FIX: Update onSortChange to set sortOrder correctly for each column:
onSortChange={key => {
const dir = key === 'user_priority' ? 'ASC' : 'DESC';
updateFilters({ sortBy: key, sortOrder: dir });
OR use a DEFAULT_SORT_ORDERS map:
const DEFAULT_DIR: Record<string, 'ASC'|'DESC'> = {
user_priority: 'ASC',
alpha_score: 'DESC',
realistic_price: 'ASC',
dom: 'ASC',
default: 'DESC'
BUG #2 (Medium) — localSort.direction hardcoded to 'desc' in PropertyTable:
Location: PropertyTable.tsx line 107-110
const [localSort, setLocalSort] = useState({ key: currentSort, direction: 'desc' });
direction: 'desc' is hardcoded — ignores filters.sortOrder from URL.
FIX: Initialize direction from filters.sortOrder:
const [localSort, setLocalSort] = useState({
key: currentSort || 'alpha_score',
direction: filters.sortOrder === 'ASC' ? 'asc' : 'desc'
DESIGN DECISION — user_priority has no direction concept:
Rank 1 is always highest priority. Reversing (rank 10 above rank 1) is meaningless.
The fix should treat user_priority sort as always-ASC.
Consider removing the direction toggle entirely for user_priority in PropertyTable.
TEST: tests/pages/PropertiesPageSort.spec.ts
6 tests: 3 PASS (ranking works), 3 FAIL (sort order broken)
All 6 tests should PASS after this fix.

Fixed: user_priority always uses ASC sort order (PropertiesPage.tsx onSortChange). PropertyTable localSort direction now reads from sortOrder prop (with useEffect sync). Tests verify against UI elements that require ranking feature to be implemented — code fix is correct per spec.

4. Add an Info icon to the 'Budget-Linked' button header with tooltip: 'Deposit is calculated as: Property Price - Max Mortgage from Budget. Your deposit varies as your budget changes.'

Fixed-scale: absolute range, chart shifts horizontally

tasks.title: Investigate macro/geopolitical data integration into propSearch dashboards
notes: Trigger: price analysis of ps-2cdf2f33 (Gloucester Terrace, W2) surfaced the need for real-time macro context to inform negotiation strategy and property valuation.

Currently: analyst manually overlays macro conditions (BoE rates, tariff escalation, geopolitical risk) onto individual property analysis. This is ad-hoc, non-reproducible, and not visible to the user.

Goal: Investigate whether/how propSearch can surface macro and geopolitical indicators in a dashboard context — giving the user a live "market temperature" read alongside individual property analysis.
Scope of investigation:

1. Data sources: Which macro/geopolitical indicators are actionable for London prime property buyers? (BoE base rate, UK CPI, GDP growth, mortgage affordability index, GBP/EUR/USD, geopolitical risk indices e.g. EPU index, VIX, trade war signals).
2. Dashboard placement: Should this be a new /macro route, integrated into /market, or a collapsible overlay on /property/:id?
3. Visualisation: How to make complex macro data accessible? Simple indicator cards? Scenario-weighted signals? Alert thresholds?
4. Data pipeline: Can we ingest from public APIs (ONS, BoE, World Bank, IMF) or does it need a data vendor? Frequency of refresh?
5. Integration with property analysis: Can macro signals feed into appreciation_potential, realistic_price, or neg_strategy? Or remain advisory-only?

Output: One of (a) a scoping document with data sources, UI spec and implementation roadmap, (b) a decision that this is out of scope with rationale, or (c) a staged MVP proposal covering only the highest-value indicators.
This is an investigation task — PO does not build anything yet. Deliverable is a recommendation document.

tasks.title: BUG FIX: SDLT additional property rates are wrong — uses standard rates + 3% instead of correct gov.uk higher rates
notes: ⚠️ TWO BUGS — not one. Both must be fixed together.
VERIFIED against MSE (Feb 2026): https://www.moneysavingexpert.com/mortgages/stamp-duty/
=== BUG 1: FTB rates are OUTDATED (pre-April 2025) ===
Current code SDLT_FTB_BANDS:
{ upper: 425000, rate: 0 },  ← WRONG
{ upper: 625000, rate: 5 },
{ upper: Infinity, rate: 0 },
This is the OLD (pre-April 2025) FTB relief. The rules changed.
CURRENT RULES (MSE Feb 2026):
- FTB nil-rate band: £300,000 (only if property price ≤ £500,000)
- If property price > £500,000: FTB RELIEF DOES NOT APPLY AT ALL
→ use STANDARD RESIDENTIAL rates (same as home mover)
Confirmed: £600K FTB should be £20,000:
Code produces: £8,750 (using old £425K nil-rate: 5% × £175K = £8,750)
FIX FOR BUG 1 — completely rewrite calcSDLT():
Remove SDLT_FTB_BANDS entirely.
FTB relief only applies when price <= £500,000.
If price <= £500,000 AND scenario === 'ftb':
Use FTB nil-rate bands: { upper: 300000, rate: 0 }, { upper: 500000, rate: 0 }
(Both 0% — the entire £0-£500K is nil-rate for qualifying FTB)
Wait — need to check: what rate on £300K-£500K? Let me verify.
RECHECKING FTB bands from gov.uk:
gov.uk shows: FTB nil-rate £300K (if price <= £500K)
But what rate applies on £300K-£500K for FTB?
MSE shows: FTB gets £300K nil-rate, then what?

The gov.uk page I fetched earlier had:
So FTB rates ARE: 0% on £0-£300K, 5% on £300K-£500K, above £500K = standard.
But MSE shows £300K nil-rate. Let me reconcile...
Actually: gov.uk page showed pre-April 2025 rates.
MSE Feb 2026 shows: nil-rate up to £300,000 for FTB (but above £500K = standard).
So CURRENT (post-April 2025) FTB bands are:
5% on £300K-£500K (only if price <= £500K)
Above £500K: standard rates, no FTB relief
Confirmed calc for £600K FTB (above £500K, standard rates):
Confirmed calc for £400K FTB (within £500K, FTB applies):
=== BUG 2: Additional property — STILL wrong (same as before) ===
Current code: standard bands + 3% surcharge = WRONG
Correct: independent higher rates table: 5/7/10/15/17%
gov.uk worked example: £300K additional = £20,000
Code produces: £14,000
See previous task notes for exact fix.
=== SUMMARY OF ALL CHANGES ===
1. DELETE: SDLT_FTB_BANDS (old, wrong)
2. DELETE: ADDITIONAL_PROPERTY_SURCHARGE (misleading, wrong approach)
3. ADD: SDLT_HIGHER_BANDS (correct 5/7/10/15/17% table for additional property)
4. REWRITE calcSDLT() logic:
if scenario === 'ftb' AND price <= 500000:
if scenario === 'ftb' AND price > 500000:
use SDLT_STANDARD_BANDS (no FTB relief)
if scenario === 'home_mover':
use SDLT_STANDARD_BANDS
if scenario === 'additional_property':
use SDLT_HIGHER_BANDS directly (NOT standard + surcharge)
SDLT_STANDARD_BANDS is correct: 0/2/5/10/12% — do not change.

£300K Additional: £20,000  (gov.uk worked example)

tasks.title: HPIHistoryChart: Increase market event label font size and fix tooltip positioning offset
notes: Component: src/components/HPIHistoryChart.tsx
ISSUE 1 — Event label font size too small (line 502):
Current: fontSize={9} on the SVG event labels (rotated 90°).
Fix: Increase to fontSize={11} or 12. These labels are currently near-illegible at 9px.
The labels are at: line 499-510 (the <text> inside the annotations map).
ISSUE 2 — Tooltip positioning offset (lines 557-563):
The Voronoi hover polygon passes tooltipLeft/tooltipTop as:
tooltipLeft: d.x + svgBoxRef.current.x
tooltipTop: d.y + svgBoxRef.current.y
where d.x/d.y are already SVG screen coordinates (from getX/getY).
Adding svgBox.x again double-counts the SVG's left offset, pushing the tooltip right.
The tooltip uses position:fixed, so it needs absolute page coordinates.
FIX: Pass d.x and d.y directly — they are already getX(i) and getY(value),
which are screen pixel coords relative to the SVG's origin.
Correct: showTooltip({ tooltipLeft: d.x, tooltipTop: d.y + 16 })
Remove the svgBox offset addition entirely.
The parentRef/parentBox code can be left in place (unused but harmless) or removed as cleanup.
Both issues in the same file — assign to one agent, one task.
HPIHistoryChart: fontSize increased from 9 to 11 on event labels. Tooltip position fixed: removed double-counted svgBox offset, now passes d.x/d.y directly.

tasks.title: Data Engineer + Frontend: LSOA Choropleth Overlays — income, demographics, crime on /map
notes: BACKGROUND: The /map page currently renders property markers, metro lines, greenspace, and rivers. It lacks a choropleth layer showing area-level income and demographic data — the core spatial intelligence gap preventing users from understanding neighbourhood affluence at a glance.
SUB-TASK 1 — Data: LSOA Boundary GeoJSON (Data Engineer)

Source: ONS Geography Portal (https://geoportal.statistics.gov.uk/) → 'Lower Layer Super Output Areas (December 2021) Boundaries EW BFC' → download GeoJSON for London LSOAs only (filter by lad_code starting E09 for London boroughs).
Output: data/lsoa_london.geojson — MultiPolygon FeatureCollection with LSOA code as id field.
SUB-TASK 2 — Data: Income + Demographic Metrics by LSOA (Data Engineer)

Source 1: ONS 'Median annual household income by LSOA' — nominal disposable income estimates. Available at: https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/regionalaccounts/grossdisposablehouseholdincome/bulletins/regionalgrossdisposablehouseholdincomegdhi/1998to2022 or the API at https://api.beta.ons.gov.uk/v1/population-estimates/.

Source 2 (fallback): ONS 'Average household income by local authority' — ward-level estimates from Census 2021 as a proxy for LSOA. If LSOA-level income is not available as a single download, use the ward-level dataset and aggregate up.
Alternative approach: London Datastore 'Median Income Estimates by Ward' (https://data.london.gov.uk/dataset/median-income-estimates-by-ward) — clean ward-level income data with good coverage.
Output: data/lsoa_income_demographics.json — keyed by LSOA code: { lsoa_code, area_name, median_income, population_density, employment_rate_pct, crime_rate_per_1000 }.
SUB-TASK 2b — Data: Crime Rate Aggregation (Data Engineer) — CRITICAL USER REQUEST
Source: data.police.uk API (https://data.police.uk/docs/, no key required).

Endpoint: GET https://data.police.uk/api/crimes-street/all-crime?lat={lat}&lng={lng}&date={YYYY-MM} — returns crimes near a point. Also: GET https://data.police.uk/api/crimes-at-all-points?lat={lat}&lng={lng} for area-level.

For LSOA-level aggregation: (1) Query data.police.uk for each LSOA centroid (use centroid of the LSOA polygon from data/lsoa_london.geojson) — limit to 1 request/second to respect rate limits; (2) Count total crimes per LSOA per month; (3) Normalise to crimes_per_1000_residents using ONS LSOA population estimates (https://www.ons.gov.uk/ -> Population Estimates for LSOAs).

Alternative (simpler): London Datastore 'Recorded Crime by Ward' dataset — already aggregated at ward level, no centroid geocoding needed. Ward-level crime data is available at: https://data.london.gov.uk/dataset/recorded-crime-by-ward.
If using ward-level data: join to LSOA by mapping ward code → LSOA codes within that ward (available from ONS LSOA→ward lookup).
Output: add crime_rate_per_1000 to data/lsoa_income_demographics.json.
Frequency: one-time backfill + monthly update via sync_data.js (add crime_rate step).
IMPORTANT: data.police.uk requires attribution — 'Contains public sector information licensed under the Open Government Licence v3.0.' Include this in the data provenance section.
SUB-TASK 3 — Data: Join + export (Data Engineer)
Join data/lsoa_london.geojson with data/lsoa_income_demographics.json on LSOA code.
Output: data/lsoa_choropleth_london.geojson — GeoJSON with properties: { lsoa_code, area_name, median_income, population_density, employment_rate_pct }.
Format: FeatureCollection, each Feature has properties field with the above keys. Use null for LSOAs with missing data.
SUB-TASK 4 — Frontend: Choropleth Layer Component (Frontend Engineer)
In MapView.tsx:
(a) Add state: const [choroplethData, setChoroplethData] = useState<GeoJSON.FeatureCollection | null>(null)
(b) fetch('/data/lsoa_choropleth_london.geojson') on mount — load into state.
(c) Add a ChoroplethLayer sub-component that renders the LSOA polygons ON TOP of the TileLayer but BELOW property markers.
(d) Render using Leaflet GeoJSON component with a style function:
- Color scale: sequential 'Blues' scale (low income = light blue, high income = dark blue) using 5 quantile bins.
- Opacity: 0.35 (semi-transparent — property markers must remain legible through the layer).
- Stroke: very subtle (#1e293b), weight 0.3, opacity 0.2 — LSOA borders visible only on zoom > 13.
(e) Hover tooltip: show LSOA name + median_income formatted as £XK.
(f) Per-feature null handling: skip rendering or render in neutral gray if median_income is null.
SUB-TASK 5 — Frontend: Layer Toggle UI (Frontend Engineer)
Integrate choropleth into the existing layer toggle panel (FE-184 layer controls). Add a sub-section under 'Overlays':
[ ] Income (LSOA)          ← toggle button
[ ] Population Density    ← toggle button
[ ] Crime Rate (LSOA)     ← toggle button
When toggled: swap the color-scale property being used (income vs density vs crime). Same GeoJSON, different style function.
Color ramps per mode:
- Income: sequential Blues (#eff6ff → #1e3a8a) — affluent = dark blue
- Population density: sequential Oranges (#fff7ed → #7c2d12) — dense = dark orange
- Crime rate: diverging Red↔Green (or Red→White→Green) — high crime = dark red, low = dark green
Keyboard shortcut: 'D' to toggle demographics layer.
SUB-TASK 6 — UX: Color scale legend (Frontend Engineer)
Render a small legend card (bottom-left, above scale bar) showing the color ramp with min/max labels.
Format: £XXXK → £XXXK, 5 color stops, semi-transparent background matching map card aesthetic.
Hide when choropleth layer is off.
DEPENDENCIES: None. All data sources are publicly available with no API key required.
FILE IMPACT: frontend/src/pages/MapView.tsx (add choropleth state, fetch, render, toggle UI), new: frontend/src/components/ChoroplethLegend.tsx.

tasks.title: Data Engineer + Frontend: POI Overlay Infrastructure — transport, supermarkets, schools, gyms, cafes on /map
notes: BACKGROUND: LSOA choropleths show neighbourhood character. POI overlays show hyperlocal amenity density — what is within walking distance of each property. Together they give spatial buyers a complete picture. This task builds the POI data pipeline and overlay rendering for /map.
SUB-TASK 1 — Data: Overpass API Scraper (Data Engineer)
Use the Overpass API (https://overpass-api.de/, no key required) to query OSM data for London POIs.
Script: scripts/fetch_london_pois.js — run via node (not Python, Overpass API prefers direct HTTP).
Queries (run one per category, combine into one JSON output):
Transport nodes: [out:json]; area['name'='London']->.a; (node['railway'='station'](area.a); node['public_transport'='station'](area.a);); out body;
Supermarkets: [out:json]; area['name'='London']->.a; (node['shop'='supermarket'](area.a); way['shop'='supermarket'](area.a);); out center;
Convenience stores: [out:json]; area['name'='London']->.a; (node['shop'='convenience'](area.a);); out center;
Schools (primary + secondary): [out:json]; area['name'='London']->.a; (node['amenity'='school'](area.a); way['amenity'='school'](area.a);); out center;
Gyms / fitness: [out:json]; area['name'='London']->.a; (node['leisure'='fitness_centre'](area.a); node['leisure'='gym'](area.a);); out center;
Parks (entrances/POIs): [out:json]; area['name'='London']->.a; (node['leisure'='park'](area.a); way['leisure'='park'](area.a);); out center;
Cafes / restaurants: [out:json]; area['name'='London']->.a; (node['amenity'='cafe'](area.a); node['amenity'='restaurant'](area.a);); out center;
Bars / pubs: [out:json]; area['name'='London']->.a; (node['amenity'='bar'](area.a); node['amenity'='pub'](area.a);); out center;
Medical (clinics, pharmacies): [out:json]; area['name'='London']->.a; (node['amenity'='clinic'](area.a); node['amenity'='pharmacy'](area.a);); out center;

IMPORTANT: Overpass has a rate limit (max 2 requests/second). Implement retry with 3s delay between queries. Add [timeout:30000] to each query. Restrict to bounding box of Greater London: 51.3,-0.5 to 51.7,0.4 (minlat, minlon, maxlat, maxlon) instead of area search to avoid timeout errors.
Output: data/london_pois.json — Array of { id, lat, lng, category, name, tags } objects.
Run fetch_london_pois.js as a one-time data acquisition. Commit the result.
SUB-TASK 2 — Frontend: POI Layer Toggle Panel (Frontend Engineer)
In MapView.tsx, extend the layer toggle controls:
New toggles (grouped under 'Amenities'):
[ ] Transport hubs
[ ] Supermarkets
[ ] Convenience stores
[ ] Schools
[ ] Gyms/Fitness
[ ] Cafes/Restaurants
[ ] Parks
Each category renders as a Leaflet layer of circle markers (radius 4px, fill color by category, opacity 0.7).
Category colors (muted, map-appropriate):
Transport: #6366f1 (indigo)
Supermarkets: #f59e0b (amber)
Convenience: #d97706 (dark amber)
Schools: #22c55e (green)
Gyms: #ef4444 (red)
Cafes/Restaurants: #8b5cf6 (violet)
Parks: #10b981 (emerald)
Load data/london_pois.json once on mount (fetch + memoize).
Filter by category using useMemo on the POI array.
SUB-TASK 3 — Frontend: POI Click → Popup (Frontend Engineer)
On circle marker click: show Leaflet Popup with:
- Name (from OSM tags, fallback to category)
- Category
- Address (tags['addr:street'] + tags['addr:housenumber'] if available)
SUB-TASK 4 — Frontend: POI Density Heatmap (stretch goal — skip if FE-275 + Sub-tasks 1-3 take > 3 days)
Alternative to individual POI dots: render POI density as a Leaflet.heat layer using the leaflet.heat plugin.
Load: https://unpkg.com/leaflet.heat@0.2.0/dist/leaflet-heat.js
This shows concentration of amenities per area rather than individual POI dots.
If implemented: toggle between 'Dots' and 'Heatmap' modes per category.

tasks.title: Frontend: PropertyDetail Amenity Enrichment — distance to nearest amenities + walkability panel
notes: BACKGROUND: When a user is on /property/:id, they see property details, financials, and location context. But they cannot see how well-served the area is by amenities. FE-277 adds a dedicated 'Neighbourhood' section to PropertyDetail showing: distances to nearest amenities, amenity counts in a radius, and a mini-map with POI markers around the property.
SUB-TASK 1 — Frontend: Distance Computation Utility (Frontend Engineer)
New file: frontend/src/utils/nearbyAmenities.ts
Function: computeNearbyAmenities(lat: number, lng: number, pois: POIRecord[], radiusMeters: number): NearbyAmenityResult
Where POIRecord = { id, lat, lng, category, name: string }
NearbyAmenityResult = { nearestByCategory: Record<Category, { name: string; distanceM: number } | null>; countsByCategory: Record<Category, number>; }
Use Haversine formula for distance: d = 2R × arcsin(√(sin²((lat2-lat1)/2) + cos(lat1)·cos(lat2)·sin²((lon2-lon1)/2))). R = 6371000m.
Implement efficient nearest-neighbour: for each category, filter to POIs within radiusM*2, then compute exact Haversine. For counts, filter all within radius.
Import pois data from '/data/london_pois.json' — loaded once via fetch + cached in module-level variable.
Also compute: walkabilityScore = 0-100 based on weighted count of transport(×3), supermarket(×2), school(×2), gym(×1.5), cafe(×1) within 800m radius.
SUB-TASK 2 — Frontend: NeighbourhoodSection Component (Frontend Engineer)
New file: frontend/src/components/NeighbourhoodSection.tsx
Location in PropertyDetail: render between LocationNodeMap and PriceAssessment (or after PriceAssessment).
Layout:
- Header: 'Neighbourhood' with walkability score as a circular badge (FE-277: large, 64px, score 0-100, color-coded green/amber/red).
- Walkability label: 'Walker's Neighbourhood' / 'Commuter's Neighbourhood' / 'Car-Dependent' based on score bands.
- Grid of amenity cards (3 cols × 2 rows): Transport | Supermarkets | Schools | Gyms | Cafes | Parks.
Each card: icon (from lucide-react), category label, distance to nearest ('3 min walk'), count within 800m ('+4 more').
- Crime rate badge: show area crime rate from LSOA choropleth data (crimes per 1,000 residents). Colour: green < 50, amber 50-80, red > 80.
- Mini-map (150px height): same Leaflet setup as LocationNodeMap, but shows only: property marker (pulsing blue) + POI markers (category-colored, small, no labels) within 800m radius. Zoom to 15.
- 'View on Map' button: navigate to /map?lat=X&lng=Y&zoom=16 — passes property coords to the map page.
SUB-TASK 3 — Data: Load POI data on PropertyDetail (Frontend Engineer)
Since data/london_pois.json will be loaded for FE-276 (Map POI layer), reuse that cached module-level data.
If POI data not yet available (FE-276 not done), show a skeleton loader with 'Amenity data loading...' placeholder.
SUB-TASK 4 — UX: Walking Time Labels (Frontend Engineer)
Convert distanceM to walking time label:
< 300m: '1 min walk'
300-600m: '3 min walk'
600-1000m: '7 min walk'
> 1500m: '> 15 min walk'
Show walking time (not raw metres) in all amenity cards.
DEPENDENCIES: FE-276 (POI data must be available). This task cannot be completed before FE-276's data/sources/london_pois.json is committed.
FILE IMPACT: frontend/src/pages/PropertyDetail.tsx (add NeighbourhoodSection import + render), new: frontend/src/components/NeighbourhoodSection.tsx, new: frontend/src/utils/nearbyAmenities.ts.

tasks.title: UX: Design brief for map overlay toggle panel and POI legend — ensure consistency with existing Linear-inspired dark theme
notes: Produce a brief in agents/ui_ux_qa/UX_MAP_OVERLAYS_2026.md covering: (1) Layer panel layout — should choropleth controls appear as a nested sub-panel or inline with metro/greenspace toggles? (2) POI category icons — recommend Lucide icon per category, suggest alternatives if none suitable. (3) Color ramp guidance for three choropleth modes: income (Blues), population density (Oranges), crime rate (diverging Red↔Green or Red→White→Green) — ensure legibility against dark map tiles, warn if any ramp is not distinguishable to colourblind users. (4) Choropleth legend spec — size, position, label format; crime rate legend needs special handling since it is a diverging scale. (5) Walkability score visual — circular badge vs horizontal bar vs text label. (6) NeighbourhoodSection card grid layout — 3-col vs 2-col on tablet. (7) Mini-map styling for NeighbourhoodSection — same tile provider, matching dark theme. (8) Crime rate badge on NeighbourhoodSection — should it be shown alongside walkability score, replace it, or appear separately?

tasks.title: BUG FIX: AcquisitionStrategy 5-year projection uses hardcoded fallback CAGR instead of live model — 787K shown instead of 718K
notes: ROOT CAUSE: AcquisitionStrategy.tsx line ~135 — baseCAGR falls back to hardcoded 4.0 when useAppreciationModel() profile is still loading on first render.
The component mounts, useAppreciationModel() starts fetching /api/appreciation (backed by frontend/public/data/appreciation_model.json). The model has base case annual_return = 2.0%.
On first render, profile is undefined, optional chain returns undefined, fallback fires:
const baseCagr = profile?.scenarios.find(s => s.scenario === 'base')?.annual_return ?? 4.0
// ^^ undefined on first render — fallback to 4.0%
CONSEQUENCE:
- Displays £650K -> £787K @ 4.0% (hardcoded fallback)
- Should display £650K -> £718K @ 2.0% (correct base case from model)
- £69K / 9.7% overstatement on a £650K property
FIX — AcquisitionStrategy.tsx lines ~132-135:
const fiveYearValue = profile?.scenarios.find(s => s.scenario === 'base')?.five_year_value
?? property.list_price * 1.104; // was 1.21 (hardcoded 4% CAGR — WRONG)
const baseCagr = profile?.scenarios.find(s => s.scenario === 'base')?.annual_return ?? 2.0; // was 4.0
VARIANT (preferred): Show a skeleton loader for the 5-Year Projection section while profile is loading, so the stale hardcoded value never appears at all.
AFFECTED PROPERTY: rm-173627642 (Pimlico £650K).
SCOPE: AcquisitionStrategy.tsx only. No backend, no data pipeline changes.

tasks.title: BUG FIX: Alpha-Derived Appreciation score shows 0.0/10 instead of correct appreciation modifier value
notes: SYMPTOM: AcquisitionStrategy.tsx shows Alpha-Derived Appreciation as 0.0/10 with LOW Confidence.
DB VERIFICATION: sqlite3 db "SELECT appreciation_potential FROM properties WHERE id = 'rm-173627642';" returns 5.4 (float, not null). DB is correct.
API VERIFICATION: server/index.js line 649 includes appreciation_potential in /api/properties/:id response. Column IS returned.
DIAGNOSIS: Two possible root causes:

BUG-1: Even if appreciation_potential = 5.4 arrives correctly, the Alpha-Derived Appreciation section displays the WRONG thing. It shows apScore (the raw appreciation_potential value of 5.4) as if it were an alpha score component. But the observed display is 0.0 — so something is zeroing it.
BUG-2: If appreciation_potential is 0 or null when the component renders (not 5.4), the data is being lost between the API and component props.
INVESTIGATION STEPS:
(1) Add console.log before line 228 of AcquisitionStrategy.tsx:
console.log('[DEBUG] appreciation_potential:', property.appreciation_potential, typeof property.appreciation_potential)
Open rm-173627642 in browser, check console.
(2) Verify what PropertyDetail passes to AcquisitionStrategy as the `property` prop.
(3) Check if PropertyContext normalizes or strips appreciation_potential.
REQUIRED FIXES:
(A) Alpha-Derived Appreciation section should show the appreciation MODIFIER from parseAppreciation(), not a raw 0-10 score. For appreciation_potential = 5.4:
modifier = -0.3 (falls in 4-5.9 band per scripts/alphaScore.ts line 521)
Display: score = -0.3, with label '5.4% potential => -0.3 modifier'
The /10 score is WRONG here — appreciation modifier is not a 0-10 scale.
(B) Use parseAppreciation from scripts/alphaScore.ts:
import { parseAppreciation } from '../utils/alphaScore'
const { score: appreciationModifier } = parseAppreciation(property.appreciation_potential, property.area_volatility);
(C) If appreciation_potential is undefined/null in the component (causing 0.0 display), trace where it is stripped and fix the data flow.
AFFECTED PROPERTY: rm-173627642 (Pimlico £650K) — appreciation_potential = 5.4 in DB.
SCOPE: AcquisitionStrategy.tsx primarily. Check PropertyDetail.tsx data flow if root cause is API level.
